items.title: Standard essential patents (SEPs): a new board for the global geostrategic game
items.title_zh: 标准必要专利：全球地缘战略博弈的新棋盘
items.summary: Standard essential patents (SEPs) are patents that protect technologies that are indispensable for implementing a technical standard.
items.content:
If a manufacturer wants to comply with the standard —for example 4G, 5G, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, video codecs or V2X in automobiles— it cannot “design around” those patents without failing to comply. Essentiality arises within standardisation bodies (SSOs/SDOs) —ETSI, IEEE, 3GPP, ITU, ISO, among others— where companies contribute technology to define common specifications that ensure global interoperability. In exchange for having their technology incorporated into the standard, proprietors usually commit to license their SEPs under certain conditions that we call FRAND, which stands for Fair, Reasonable and Non-Discriminatory. It is a commitment that the SEP proprietor makes to the SSO and, in practice, to the market. The balance FRAND seeks to strike is a delicate one: avoiding the proprietor’s hold-up (blocking markets with an SEP) without encouraging the implementer’s hold-out (using the technology without paying, hoping to litigate later). SEPs are, therefore, a structural building block of the digital economy: they enable global markets for interoperable devices, but concentrate technological power in portfolios that can condition entire supply chains. This debate has traditionally been an issue for the telecoms sector with its 2G/3G/4G/5G, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, NFC standards, etc., but today it is a critical issue for all sectors with ”connected things”: Consumer electronics, automotive, industry, energy, etc. In particular, the expansion into automobiles and industry explains the increase in disputes and regulatory moves in recent months. Situation in the US, Asia and the EU In the United States, at the end of 2025, the United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO) announced the creation of the Standard-Essential Patent (SEP) Working Group, a cross-functional team reporting directly to the Director of the USPTO. The group is co-chaired by senior legal officials from the Office and was created with a clear narrative: to strengthen an ecosystem that, according to the USPTO itself, had become ‘hostile’ to innovators due to unclear rules and downward pressure on licence fees. As a first tangible initiative, in January 2026 the USPTO has launched the Standards Participation and Representation Kudos (SPARK) pilot programme, which grants a limited number of acceleration certificates (to speed up the review of applications or appeals) to entities that participate significantly in standardisation. In Japan, in June 2025, the Tokyo District Court issued what is considered to be the first SEP-based injunction in Japan in Pantech v. Google, ordering the cessation of sales of the Pixel 7. In this case, the Court distinguishes between two phases: a negotiation phase (where methodological disagreements are tolerated), and a conciliation phase supervised by the court, with methodology and information requirements. The turning point has been the lack of cooperation from the implementer in the supervised phase (including the refusal to provide sales data needed to verify proposals). Japan ”opens the door” to the application of injunctive relief in the case of SEP infringement, but with a demanding standard that is closely linked to procedural conduct. In turn, China has become a central forum for SEP disputes because of its willingness to set global royalties and its use of procedural tools such as anti-suit injunctions (ASIs). This has generated friction with other jurisdictions and has led to an international trade dimension: the EU took China to the WTO over practices related to setting global royalties, and the TRIPS compatibility debate has become part of the geopolitical chessboard of SEPs. In 2023, the European Commission presented an ambitious proposal for a Regulation on SEPs and FRAND (transparency, registration, verification of essentiality and specific procedures) promoted by the European automotive industry. Following strong opposition from industry and Member States, the proposal was withdrawn. The political reading is two-fold: on the one hand, the need for more transparency and predictability in the SEP market is recognised. On the other hand, there is caution about the creation of mandatory procedures that could make licensing more expensive and slower, affecting European competitiveness. The result is a period of rethinking. It is plausible that the EU will return with a more modular reform (registration, reporting and best practices) that is less interventionist in economic negotiations. At the same time, the Commission is using trade (WTO) to address the extraterritorial effects of SEP litigation. The challenge of the next decade will be to preserve the incentive to contribute technology to standards —the engine of the digital economy— without allowing blocking power or strategic procrastination to distort entire markets. A large part of global technological competitiveness is at stake in this balance, and Europe must make its move in the coming months. The difficulty lies in managing the interests of all parties, both technology providers and users. Once again, patents are at the heart of the strategy. Written by: Luis Ignacio Vicente del Olmo . Strategic Advisor at PONS IP.

Standard essential patents (SEPs) are patents that protect technologies that are indispensable for implementing a technical standard. If a manufacturer wants to comply with the standard —for example 4G, 5G, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, video codecs or V2X in automobiles— it cannot “design around” those patents without failing to comply.

Essentiality arises within standardisation bodies (SSOs/SDOs) —ETSI, IEEE, 3GPP, ITU, ISO, among others— where companies contribute technology to define common specifications that ensure global interoperability. In exchange for having their technology incorporated into the standard, proprietors usually commit to license their SEPs under certain conditions that we call FRAND, which stands for Fair, Reasonable and Non-Discriminatory. It is a commitment that the SEP proprietor makes to the S
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文剖析标准必要专利（SEP）作为数字经济基石的地缘战略意义，指出其已从电信业扩展至汽车等互联领域。文章对比了美、亚、欧三地动态：美国成立工作组并推出加速计划以扶持创新；日本法院作出首例 SEP 禁令；欧盟则面临平衡 FRAND 承诺与防止市场滥用的监管挑战，凸显全球规则博弈加剧。

items.title: JUB – Compétence confirmée dans l’affaire Sun Patent Trust c/ Vivo
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院巴黎分院确认 Sun 诉 Vivo 案管辖权
items.summary: JUB : la division locale de Paris a rendu une ordonnance rejetant l’exception d’incompétence soulevée par Vivo dans le litige l’opposant à Sun Patent Trust.
items.content:
Le 30 octobre 2025, la division locale de Paris de la Juridiction unifiée du brevet (JUB) a rendu une ordonnance (UPC_CFI_361/2025) rejetant l’exception d’incompétence soulevée par Vivo dans le litige l’opposant à Sun Patent Trust.

L’affaire concerne le brevet européen EP 3 852 468, relatif aux technologies 4G+, dont Sun Patent Trust reproche la contrefaçon par la commercialisation de divers modèles de smartphones Vivo en Europe.

Vivo soutenait que la JUB n’était pas compétente pour statuer sur la détermination des conditions FRAND, présentées par le demandeur comme partie intégrante de son action, et contestait également la compétence territoriale de la division parisienne.

Sun Patent Trust arguait au contraire qu’il s’agissait d’une action en contrefaçon de brevet essentiel (SEP), relevant de l’article 32(1)(a) UPCA, et que la question des conditions FRAND n’était pas une demande autonome mais une condition préalable à l’octroi d’une injonction.

Les magistrats de la division locale ont considéré que l’action principale était bien une action en contrefaçon et que les questions FRAND pouvaient être examinées à titre incident, conformément à la jurisprudence de la JUB et à l’arrêt Huawei v. ZTE. L’admissibilité de la sous-demande relative aux conditions FRAND sera tranchée au fond.

Sur le plan territorial, la Cour a jugé que l’achat d’un produit Vivo sur le site Fnac.com, destiné au marché français, suffisait à établir la compétence de la division locale de Paris au sens de l’article 33(1)(a) UPCA.

Cette ordonnance confirme la compétence de la JUB pour traiter les litiges SEP/FRAND dans le cadre d’actions en contrefaçon et illustre une approche pragmatique de la compétence territoriale, fondée sur des actes de commercialisation en ligne. L’affaire se poursuivra désormais sur le fond, avec un débat attendu sur la conformité des offres de licence aux exigences FRAND et sur l’octroi éventuel d’une injonction contre Vivo.

items.ai_summary_zh: 2025 年 10 月，统一专利法院（UPC）巴黎本地分院驳回 Vivo 提出的管辖权异议。法院认定，涉及标准必要专利（SEP）的侵权诉讼中，FRAND 许可条件可作为附带问题审理，且通过 Fnac 网站向法国销售产品足以确立地域管辖。此案确认了 UPC 处理 SEP/FRAND 纠纷的权限及基于在线销售的管辖原则。

items.title: Bristows’ SnippITs - Under construction: Court of Appeal infers termination rights in Zaha Hadid dispute
items.title_zh: 上诉法院在扎哈·哈迪德案中推断合理通知终止权
items.summary: This post is the latest in the Bristows’ SnippITs series, which pulls together the key practical takeaways from recent court decisions for the tech sect...
items.content: This post is the latest in the Bristows’ SnippITs series, which pulls together the key practical takeaways from recent court decisions for the tech sector and beyond. In January , we reported the judgment from the High Court in which Mr Justice Adam Johnson declined to construe a mutual right to terminate on reasonable notice into a trademark licence agreement with express, but unilateral, termination rights. On 27 February 2026, the Court of Appeal handed down judgment in Zaha Hadid Limited v The Zaha Hadid Foundation [2026] EWCA Civ 192 , reversing the High Court’s decision and inferring a mutual right to terminate on reasonable notice as a matter of construction. On review of the case law, the Court of Appeal adopted the two-stage construction logic from Winter Garden Theatre (London) Limited v Millenium Productions Ltd [1948] AC 173: 1. Did the parties intend the agreement to run in perpetuity or merely indefinitely (i.e. to be terminated at an unspecified time)? 2. (a) A perpetual agreement leaves no room to infer termination rights in the absence of express terms. (b) Conversely, to effect an intention for the agreement to be indefinite, it necessarily follows that both parties must be able to bring it to an end, i.e. to terminate on reasonable notice.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国上诉法院推翻高等法院判决，在扎哈·哈迪德商标许可纠纷中，依据合同解释原则推断出双方均享有合理通知下的相互终止权。法院采用两阶段逻辑：若协议意在无限期而非永久，则必然隐含终止权利。此裁决修正了仅凭单方明示条款排除默示终止权的观点，对技术 sector 许可协议具有深远影响。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["上诉法院推翻原判，认定无限期协议隐含合理通知终止权", "确立两阶段合同解释逻辑，区分永久性与无限期协议意图", "明确缺乏明示条款时，无限期许可仍可由双方合理终止"]
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧签署商标或技术许可时，需警惕“无限期”条款可能被解读为可随意终止。建议在合同中明确约定终止条件与通知期限，避免因法律默示规则导致商业预期落空，增强协议稳定性。
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["避免使用模糊的无限期条款，应明示终止机制以防法律推断", "复核存量许可协议，评估英国法下默示终止权带来的履约风险"]

items.title: Sanctions, service, and presence
items.title_zh: 制裁、送达与管辖：英国上诉法院重申属地存在要求
items.summary: The Court of Appeal has re-affirmed that the common law requirement of presence within the jurisdiction is a substantive jurisdictional requirement, and...
items.content: The Court of Appeal has re-affirmed that the common law requirement of presence within the jurisdiction is a substantive jurisdictional requirement, and has not been displaced by the CPR’s modernisation of service methods ( Mikhail Fridman v Agrofirma Oniks LLC and another [2026] EWCA Civ 139 ). The court held that it had no personal jurisdiction over Mr Mikhail Fridman because his leave to remain had been cancelled under the sanctions regime and he was no longer permitted to enter the UK. In reaching this conclusion, the court disagreed with the Commercial Court, which had treated his absence as temporary and accepted that service at his usual or last known address was valid. Mr Mikhail Fridman, a wealthy businessman, relocated to London in 2013, and in 2016 purchased Athlone House in Hampstead as a substantial family residence. By January 2019, he had been granted indefinite leave to remain in the United Kingdom. On 15 March 2022, Mr Fridman was designated under regulations 5 and 6 of the Russia (Sanctions) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019 (Russia Regulations) and Part 1 of the Sanctions and Anti-Money Laundering Act 2018. The designation carried two significant consequences for Mr Fridman: In August 2023, during the course of a commercial dispute, the Claimants sent a letter before action to Mr Fridman at Athlone House and he did not respond. On 27 September 2023, Mr Fridman left the UK. He nevertheless remained the beneficial owner of Athlone House, which continued to be staffed and managed on his behalf through Athlone House Limited (“AHL”). The Claimants issued proceedings on 9 February 2024, identifying Athlone House as the address for service of the claim form. Mr Fridman disputes that he was present or resident there at the relevant time. The Claimants attempted service through several methods:
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国上诉法院在弗里德曼案中重申，普通法下的“境内存在”是实质性管辖要件，未被民事诉讼规则现代化取代。因当事人受制裁取消居留许可且离境，法院认定无个人管辖权，推翻商业法院关于在其住所送达有效的裁定，明确制裁导致的缺席不构成临时性缺席。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["上诉法院确认境内存在是实质性管辖前提，不因送达方式更新而改变", "受制裁导致居留权取消及离境，使被告不再满足英国法院管辖条件", "即使拥有房产并由他人管理，实际不在境内亦无法确立有效司法管辖"]
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧涉讼需关注制裁对管辖权的阻断效应。若对方受制裁离境，传统地址送达可能无效，应提前评估管辖连接点，避免因程序瑕疵导致诉讼延误或败诉风险。
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["制裁致被告离境可阻断英国法院管辖，需重新审视诉讼策略", "仅凭房产或代理管理不足以确立管辖，须核实被告实际物理存在"]

items.title: UPC Weekly - UPC Court of Appeal sets out broad, purposive claim interpretation
items.title_zh: UPC 上诉院确立宽泛目的性权利要求解释原则
items.summary: Matthew Naylor reviews the UPC Court of Appeal claim interpretation in Dyson v. Dreame, explaining how the substantive scope of the preliminary injunction was broadened to capture more products.
items.content: The referral to the CJEU by the UPC Court of Appeal (CoA) in Dyson v. Dreame – CJEU referral rightly got a lot of attention last week, including by us in UPC Weekly 2026 w10 . But the reality is that the CJEU will not answer the questions about UPC jurisdiction quickly. Conscious of this, the CoA dealt with everything but the trickiest jurisdictional questions by way of final decision. The substantive aspects of the CoA decision on the preliminary injunction (PI) are set out in Dyson v. Dreame – substantive infringement . The CoA completely reviewed the first instance decision and overturned significant aspects. The end result is a materially broader claim interpretation and further guidance on the utility of the file wrapper in UPC proceedings. EP 3119235 B1 was granted in April 2025 after a long process of examination by the EPO involving many sets of observations on patentability filed by third parties, which interestingly relied exclusively on prior art originating from the patent proprietor. The patent has since been opposed at the EPO, but the opposition is still at an early stage. The patent relates to an attachment for a hot styling brush hair care appliance. Claim 1 is below. The features needing most attention for claim interpretation are shown in bold. 1. An attachment (30) for a hand held appliance comprising a body having a wall, a fluid inlet at one end of the wall and a fluid outlet through the wall, wherein: the fluid outlet comprises a slot (102, 202, 282) extending along the wall, the slot (102, 202, 282) is formed by an overlap of a first end of the wall and a second end of the wall , the attachment (30) is tubular, the slot (102, 202, 282) extends longitudinally along the tubular attachment (30), hair is wrapped around the attachment (30) in the direction of fluid flow, the fluid emitted from the fluid outlet is attracted to an external surface (112) of the wall, and fluid emitted from the fluid outlet flows around the external surface (112) of the wall. The attachment causes air to flow out of the slot circumferentially around the outside of the tubular attachment by a clever bit of fluid dynamics and this causes hair to wrap around with the airflow. In the embodiment shown below, the slots 102 are formed by overlapping adjacent plates 110a, 110b. At first instance, the alleged infringing products from Dreame were referred to as the AirStyle Pro, the Pocket Neo (shown below), the AirStyle and the Pocket (shown below). As we explained in UPC Weekly 2025 w34 , the Hamburg LD carried out a detailed interpretation of the features of the claim defining the slot, from which the air flows to wrap, dry and style hair. They considered whether it was appropriate to refer to the file wrapper from EPO prosecution. Following the approach of the UPC CoA in Alexion v. Samsung (see also UPC Weekly 2025 w2 ), the LD stated that the granting procedure is not by itself relevant for the interpretation of the claim. However, the patentee’s assertions during the grant proceedings, and the agreement of the EPO examining division with these assertions, can be seen as an indication of the view of the person skilled in the art at the filing date. The court was happy to give various features of the claim a functional or purposive interpretation, such as “overlap” and “longitudinal”. However, the reference to “ends” of the wall was taken to limit the claim, in part based on the discussion of the scope of the claim in the file wrapper. The effect of this was that the new products (the Dreame AirStyle Pro and Pocket Neo) were considered not to infringe, because the air flow apertures were formed through the plates. However, the older products (the Dreame AirStyle and Pocket) were infringements, because the air flow apertures were formed at the join between adjacent plates. The difference in outcome for the older and newer products was whether the “slot” forming the fluid outlet was formed by an overlap of a first end of the wall and a second end of the wall. The Hamburg LD therefore granted the PI only in respect of the older products.

items.title: UPC Weekly - Asking the neighbours for some directions – UPC Court of Appeal makes first referral to CJEU
items.title_zh: UPC 上诉法院就长臂管辖权首次向欧盟法院提请裁决
items.summary: The UPC Court of Appeal makes the UPC’s first referral to the CJEU on the topic of long-arm jurisdiction.
items.content: The UPC Court of Appeal (CoA) and the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) are located on opposite sides of the Avenue John F. Kennedy in Luxembourg. It’s a 7-minute walk from one to the other. But it’s taken 144 weeks for the UPC to ask the CJEU for its interpretation of EU law as it applies to the implementation of the UPC Agreement. What’s the topic? Well unsurprisingly it’s the question of long arm jurisdiction. Specifically, the CoA ask for guidance on whether the UPC has jurisdiction over a non-EU based defendant for alleged infringement of a European patent in a non-UPC EU country (Spain, in this case). There are two added complexities: this is a preliminary injunction (PI) case, and the “anchor defendant” is a service provider that is required by EU product safety regulations in order for the alleged infringing product to be allowed to be sold in the EU. Cleverly, the CoA has divided its work products into two parts. In one part ( Dyson v. Dreame – CJEU referral ) they set out the jurisdictional questions on which they feel they must seek guidance from the CJEU because these require interpretation of several points of EU law. The CoA stay the UPC appeal in those respects. But in the other part ( Dyson v. Dreame – substantive infringement ), they set out their decision on the substantive questions of claim interpretation and infringement, applying these to the defendants/territories with no jurisdictional defence. This second part is not being sent to the CJEU, and is not stayed, so is final. We reviewed the Hamburg Local Division first instance decision in UPC Weekly 2025 w34 . Please read that (again) first – it sets out the rationale for the LD deciding that a PI should be granted in respect of only some of Dreame’s products and against all of the defendants inside the UPC territory and against only some of the defendants in Spain. It also foreshadows the CJEU referral. This week, we summarise the CoA explanation and the CJEU referral on the jurisdictional issues. Mindful that this article is already long, we’ll come back next week and turn to the equally interesting views of the CoA on claim interpretation, effect of the file wrapper and infringement. The defendants are referred to in the decisions as “Dreame”. There were originally 4 of them and we’ll stick with this numbering here. Dreame International (Hong Kong) Limited Offers products on official Dreame website and is the website operator of many country-specific websites “Official distributor of Dreame”, operator of https://dreame.de/ , operates retail store in Germany EU authorised representative for D1 Swedish affiliate to D1, operates www.se.dreametech.com , and runs retail store in Sweden In slightly abbreviated form, the wording of the PIs granted by the Hamburg LD went like this: D1, D2, D3, D4 are ordered, by way of preliminary injunction, Ia. For D1, D2 and D4 in the UPC states to refrain from for making, offering, placing on the market, etc., Ib. For D3 in the UPC states to refrain from providing service for making, offering, placing on the market, etc., Ic. For D1 and D3 also with respect to Spain, the product of claim 1. Dyson appealed the first instance decision because it only found infringement for one, older set of products. But Dyson did not appeal the finding that D2 and D4 did not infringe the patent in Spain.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉法院在戴森诉追觅案中，首次就长臂管辖权问题向欧盟法院（CJEU）提请初步裁决。核心争议在于 UPC 是否对非欧盟被告在非 UPC 成员国（如西班牙）的侵权行为拥有管辖权。法院将案件拆分为管辖权（暂停审理等待裁定）与实体侵权（已作终局判决）两部分处理，此举将确立欧洲专利跨境执法的关键先例。

items.title: Dyson v Dreame: The long-arm of the UPC, and its limits – the UPC's first CJEU referral
items.title_zh: 戴森诉追觅案：UPC 长臂管辖界限及首提 CJEU 裁决
items.summary: On 6 March 2026, the UPC Court of Appeal issued a landmark order in Dyson v Dreame & Eurep referring four questions concerning the extent of the UPC’s l...
items.content: On 6 March 2026, the UPC Court of Appeal issued a landmark order in Dyson v Dreame & Eurep referring four questions concerning the extent of the UPC’s long-arm jurisdiction to the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) – making it the UPC’s first ever referral to the CJEU. Dyson is the proprietor of EP 3 119 235 (the Patent) relating to a handheld hair care appliance. On 2 May 2025, Dyson applied for a preliminary injunction at the UPC’s Hamburg Local Division against four defendants: (i) Dreame International (Hongkong) Limited (Dreame) – a Chinese consumer electronics company that manufactured and sold appliances (including multi-functional hairdryers) via its websites in Germany and Spain; (ii) Eurep GmbH (Eurep) – Dreame’s Authorised Representative under the EU product safety regulations; (iii) Teqphone GmbH – Dreame’s German distributor; and (iv) Dreame Technology AB – Dreame’s Swedish distributor. Dyson alleged of its Patent by the defendants across all UPC member states and in Spain (a non-UPC state). Notably, Eurep did not sell or distribute Dreame’s products. Its sole function was to act as Dreame’s Authorised Representative – a role mandated by EU product safety regulations (Regulations 2023/988 and 2019/1020) for non-EU manufacturers wishing to place products on the EU market. In practice, this meant acting as the formal contact point for consumers and authorities, and doing other administrative tasks under those regulations, including storing technical documentation. At first instance, the Hamburg Local Division found that some of the Dreame products complained of likely infringed the Patent and therefore issued a preliminary injunction against the defendants. The preliminary injunction was extended to Spain with respect to Dreame and Eurep. Both Dyson and Dreame appealed against the Hamburg Local Division’s order. Dyson argued that the preliminary injunction should extend to all the Dreame products complained of. Dreame, on the other hand, argued that the preliminary injunction should be set aside in its entirety. The UPC Court of Appeal held that the appeals raised two distinct sets of issues. In respect of the action against Dreame concerning the UPC territory, and the action against Teqphone and Dreame Technology, the Court of Appeal found that no questions of EU law arose and accordingly proceeded to determine those parts of the appeal. On those issues, the Court of Appeal sided with Dyson – extending the preliminary injunction to cover the Dreame products complained of and dismissing Dreame's cross-appeal.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉庭在戴森诉追觅案中，就其长臂管辖权范围首次向欧盟法院（CJEU）提请初步裁决。案件核心争议在于非欧盟制造商的授权代表是否因履行产品安全法规义务而构成专利侵权主体。上诉庭暂维持对制造商禁令，但将授权代表责任等关键法律问题移交 CJEU 定夺。

items.title: The first CJEU referral from the UPC
items.title_zh: UPC 上诉法院就长臂管辖问题首次向 CJEU 提请初步裁决
items.summary: It appears that the CJEU now has another opportunity to have its say on the issue of long-arm jurisdiction. This time as a result of an order issued by ...
items.content: It appears that the CJEU now has another opportunity to have its say on the issue of long-arm jurisdiction. This time as a result of an order issued by the UPC Court of Appeal on 6 March in Dyson v Dreame & Eurep case, in which it stayed the appeal proceedings insofar as they related to the territory of Spain and Eurep (the Authorised Representative for manufacturers based in a non-EU Member State); and requested that the CJEU give a preliminary ruling on 4 questions relating to long arm jurisdiction and anchor defendants.
items.ai_summary_zh: 在戴森诉追觅及 Eurep 案中，统一专利法院（UPC）上诉法院于 3 月 6 日发布命令，暂停涉及西班牙领土及非欧盟制造商授权代表的上诉程序。法院正式请求欧盟法院（CJEU）就长臂管辖权及锚定被告问题作出四项初步裁决。此举标志着 UPC 首次启动向 CJEU 的转介程序，旨在厘清跨境专利侵权诉讼中的关键管辖权界限。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 上诉法院在戴森案中首次暂停部分程序并转介 CJEU", "核心争议聚焦于针对非欧盟实体的长臂管辖权适用范围", "CJEU 的初步裁决将确立 UPC 体系下锚定被告的法律标准"]
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海企业需高度关注此案，因其直接决定非欧盟企业在欧被诉风险。若 CJEU 确认宽泛管辖权，中国企业即便无欧盟实体也可能面临诉讼。建议提前评估欧洲业务架构，制定应对长臂管辖的合规与诉讼策略。

items.title: Local division Mannheim flirts with setting FRAND rate
items.title_zh: UPC 曼海姆分部倾向裁定 FRAND 许可费率
items.summary: Following Munich Regional Court, Mannheim local division is now also opening up to making a FRAND-rate determination.
items.content:
Firms & Experts Sponsored: Firm Profiles ARNOLD RUESS WALLINGER RICKER SCHLOTTER TOSTMANN GLAWE · DELFS · MOLL View all Firm Profiles Sponsored: Expert Profiles Yannick Schütt, LL.M. Sebastian Kratzer Yelena Morozova View all Expert Profiles Sponsored Content UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP View all Sponsored Content

WALLINGER RICKER SCHLOTTER TOSTMANN

UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP

The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

German courts have always had a reputation for avoiding FRAND-rate determinations. Until now. Following Munich Regional Court, Mannheim local division is now also opening up to this. In yesterday's hearing in ZTE vs Samsung, presiding judge Peter Tochtermann presented the framework for a UPC FRAND-rate determination proceeding.

During yesterday’s hearing, the Mannheim local division under presiding judge Peter Tochtermann took the opportunity to provide an astonishing amount of basic information that did not necessarily relate to the case being heard that day. The local division heard the first of three infringement claims at the UPC between ZTE and Samsung.

The relatively small courtroom at Schubertstraße 11 in Mannheim was filled to capacity with lawyers, patent attorneys, in-house representatives and economic experts from ZTE and Samsung. Additional employees from both companies were also present from South Korea and China. Other observers watched from the overflow room. JUVE Patent was also present.

Tochtermann encouraged those present to file lawsuits at non-German UPC divisions, such as the Paris local division. Carine Gillet from Paris is the third judge on the panel in Mannheim alongside Tochtermann. Tochtermann also had some de-escalating words about the current tense relationship with the UK High Court. Above all, however, in the panel’s preliminary opinion he addressed the topic of FRAND-rate setting — considered to be the domain of the UK High Court.

There is a specific reason for this. Samsung had filed a FRAND counterclaim in the proceedings at the end of 2025, which the local division must now decide on.
items.ai_summary_zh: 继慕尼黑地方法院后，统一专利法院（UPC）曼海姆地方分院在 ZTE 诉三星案中展现新动向。主审法官托赫特曼明确提出 FRAND 费率裁定框架，回应三星提出的反诉。此举打破德国法院长期避免裁定全球费率的惯例，显示 UPC 正积极介入标准必要专利定价核心领域，可能重塑欧洲乃至全球专利许可诉讼策略。

items.title: FRAND licence disputes did not lead to more cases at UK patent courts
items.title_zh: FRAND 许可争议未致英国专利法院案件激增
items.summary: UK figures show London's reputation for FRAND has not led to the anticipated surge in case numbers at UK patent courts.
items.content:
Firms & Experts Sponsored: Firm Profiles Prüfer and Partner mbB Kirkland & Ellis KATHER AUGENSTEIN View all Firm Profiles Sponsored: Expert Profiles Peter van Schijndel Tess Waldron Dr. Arwed Burrichter View all Expert Profiles Sponsored Content UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP View all Sponsored Content

UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP

The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

The number of cases at the UK High Court fell slightly in 2024, while the IPEC saw a modest increase in IP cases compared to the previous year. However, London's reputation for FRAND-rate determinations has not yet delivered the surge in case numbers that many UK lawyers had anticipated.

The UK High Court and Court of Appeal have established themselves globally as the go-to courts for determining FRAND rates between SEP holders and implementers. Many UK lawyers had hoped this would attract more cases and business to London firms. However, according to the UK civil justice statistics , this did not materialise in 2024.

The UK High Court recorded slightly fewer cases than in 2023, dropping from 46 to 44 new patent cases. The court reached its peak in 2016 and 2017, with 71 and 85 new cases respectively. Numbers then fell to 40 and have since hovered around 50 new cases per year. After falling to 35 in 2022, cases recovered by 31% to 46 in 2023.

Many UK lawyers saw this as evidence that the UK’s position outside the UPC was benefiting its patent courts. They had hoped this trend would continue. However, these hopes were dashed, particularly in the final two quarters of 2024 when new cases notably declined. The High Court, with permanent judges Richard Meade and James Mellor, recorded only nine new cases in each of these quarters, compared to ten and 16 in the first two quarters.

Like the High Court, the Intellectual Property Enterprise Court saw its highest number of cases in 2017, with 242 new IP cases. The IPEC, a specialist subdivision within the Business and Property Courts of the UK High Court, has since consistently recorded fewer than 200 cases annually.

Over the past twelve months, new IP cases totalled 141, up 6.9% from 132 in 2023. This marks the second consecutive year of growth. While the UK statistics do not specify how many IPEC cases relate to patents, experts believe technical property rights make up the majority.
items.ai_summary_zh: 尽管伦敦确立了全球 FRAND 费率裁决中心地位，但 2024 年英国高等法院新专利案数量微降至 44 件，未现预期增长。虽然知识产权企业法院（IPEC）案件小幅上升，但业界期望的因脱离统一专利法院而带来的业务红利并未在下半年显现，案件量反而回落。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国高等法院 2024 年新专利案件略减，未达律师界预期增量", "伦敦作为 FRAND 裁决中心的声誉尚未转化为案件数量的显著 surge", "年下半年新案明显 decline，打破此前因脱离 UPC 受益的趋势"]
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应理性看待英国法院在 SEP 领域的角色，避免盲目跟风诉讼。需关注其案件量回落背后的司法效率或策略变化，结合 UPC 动态综合评估全球诉讼布局，优化资源配置。
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["勿高估英国法院案件激增趋势，需动态评估诉讼性价比", "统筹考量 UPC 与英国法院差异，制定灵活全球应诉策略"]

items.title: Munich Higher Regional Court confirms new FRAND guidelines in VoiceAge vs HMD
items.title_zh: 慕尼黑高院确认 VoiceAge 诉 HMD 案新 FRAND 指南
items.summary: Higher Regional Court rules HMD is unwilling licensee infringing VoiceAge EVS speech-coding patent and cannot sell products in Germany.
items.content:
Firms & Experts Sponsored: Firm Profiles Prüfer and Partner mbB BARDEHLE PAGENBERG VOSSIUS & PARTNER Patentanwälte Rechtsanwälte mbB View all Firm Profiles Sponsored: Expert Profiles Christopher J. Hall, Ph.D. Dr. Christian Pioch Bernhard Thum View all Expert Profiles Sponsored Content UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP View all Sponsored Content

VOSSIUS & PARTNER Patentanwälte Rechtsanwälte mbB

UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP

The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

HMD continues to infringe a patent belonging to VoiceAge's speech-coding standard EVS and cannot distribute corresponding products in Germany. The Higher Regional Court Munich has classified HMD as an unwilling licensee in its ruling. The judges simultaneously confirmed an injunction against Oppo.

The Higher Regional Court Munich under presiding judge Lars Meinhardt has rejected HMD’s appeal against a ruling by the Regional Court Munich. The court found that HMD infringes the EVS speech-coding patent EP 2 102 619 owned by VoiceAge and is not a willing licensee (case ID: 6 U 3824/22 Kart).

Originally, the court had planned to publish its highly anticipated ruling on 6 February. However, due to the scope of the decision, the court needed six additional weeks.

Given the fundamental importance of today’s decision, the Higher Regional Court Munich has granted HMD leave to appeal to the German Federal Court of Justice. The dispute between VoiceAge and HMD has gained considerable significance for SEP and FRAND law in Germany.

In 2019, VoiceAge sued HMD over five patents in Mannheim and Munich. EP 1 509 903 , EP 2 162 880 , EP 2 707 687, EP 3 132 443 and EP 2 102 619 cover the speech-coding standard EVS.
items.ai_summary_zh: 慕尼黑高等地区法院驳回 HMD 上诉，裁定其侵犯 VoiceAge 的 EVS 语音编码标准必要专利且属非自愿被许可人。法院确认对 HMD 的销售禁令，并重申对 Oppo 的禁令。该案确立了德国 SEP 诉讼中关于许可意愿判定的新指引，具有里程碑意义，已获准上诉至联邦最高法院。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院认定 HMD 为非自愿被许可人，维持侵权判决与销售禁令", "此案确立德国 SEP 纠纷中许可意愿判定的新司法指导原则", "鉴于案件重要性，法院批准 HMD 向联邦最高法院提起上诉"]
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临更严 SEP 合规要求，需主动构建 FRAND 谈判证据链。建议提前评估专利风险，避免被认定为非自愿被许可人而遭禁售，同时关注德国司法动向以调整全球许可策略。
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["主动参与 FRAND 谈判并留存证据，避免被定性为非自愿方", "密切关注德国判例演变，及时调整欧洲市场知识产权应对策略"]
items.title_en: Munich Higher Regional Court confirms new FRAND guidelines in VoiceAge vs HMD

此案确立德国 SEP 纠纷中许可意愿判定的新司法指导原则

items.title: No stay for Huawei in UK FRAND trial against MediaTek
items.title_zh: 英国高院驳回华为中止申请，FRAND 费率案将继续推进
items.summary: The UK High Court dismisses Huawei's second bid to stay FRAND rate-setting case against MediaTek and denies leave to appeal.
items.content:
Firms & Experts Sponsored: Firm Profiles Boult Wade Tennant TALIENS Simmons + Simmons LLP View all Firm Profiles Sponsored: Expert Profiles Caroline Levesque Dr. Jeroen den Hartog Tess Waldron View all Expert Profiles Sponsored Content UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP View all Sponsored Content

UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP

The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

The global SEP dispute between Huawei and MediaTek looks set to proceed in the UK, as the Chinese mobile phone manufacturer has once again failed in its bid to suspend the FRAND case at the High Court.

The UK High Court’s refusal to suspend the case means the FRAND rate-setting proceedings for Huawei’s 5G portfolio can now continue unimpeded. High Court judge Thomas Leech also denied Huawei permission to appeal his decision (case ID: HP-2024-000028 ). However, Huawei may still petition the Court of Appeal directly for leave to appeal.

In a global SEPS dispute between the two parties, spanning China, Germany and the UPC, MediaTek sought a global FRAND-rate determination for Huawei’s patent portfolio from the UK High Court. Huawei contested the court’s jurisdiction to set a global FRAND rate, and alternatively applied for a stay on case-management grounds.

In March, judge Leech rejected this challenge, affirming the court’s competence to rule on MediaTek’s request and dismissing the stay application.

On 10 April 2025, Huawei filed a further application seeking a second case-management stay, supported by four new witness statements and a skeleton argument. Last Friday, judge Leech again declined to stay the case.

The rate-setting proceedings can now move forward. The oral hearing will take place in February 2026.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国高等法院再次驳回华为针对联发科提出的中止 FRAND 费率设定案件的请求，并拒绝其上诉许可。法官 Thomas Leech 确认法院有权裁定全球费率，驳回了华为基于案件管理的延期申请。尽管华为仍可直接向上诉法院申请许可，但原定 2026 年 2 月的口头听证会将如期进行，标志着双方全球 SEP 争端在英国司法程序中将不受阻碍地继续推进。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国高院二度驳回华为中止申请，确认对全球 FRAND 费率拥有管辖权", "法官拒绝授予上诉许可，但华为仍可尝试直接向上诉法院提出申请", "案件程序将按计划推进，定于 2026 年 2 月举行关于费率设定的口头听证"]
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临更严峻的 SEP 诉讼环境，英国法院倾向于快速推进全球费率裁决。建议企业提前布局证据，评估管辖权异议策略，并做好应对高额费率判定的合规与财务准备，避免被动局面。
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["英国法院加速推进全球费率裁决，中国企业需强化应诉准备与证据管理", "应重新评估管辖权挑战策略，并提前规划潜在高额许可费的合规应对方案"]

items.title: Munich Regional Court reinterprets FRAND rules in Samsung vs ZTE
items.title_zh: 慕尼黑地方法院在三星诉中兴案中重构 FRAND 规则
items.summary: In ZTE vs Samsung, the 7th Civil Chamber of Munich Regional Court has set out new approaches to FRAND proceedings.
items.content:
Firms & Experts Sponsored: Firm Profiles HEUKING Boult Wade Tennant Appleyard Lees View all Firm Profiles Sponsored: Expert Profiles Dr. Thomas Lynker Liesbeth Weynants Daryl Penny View all Expert Profiles Sponsored Content UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP View all Sponsored Content

UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP

The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

In the pan-European dispute between ZTE and Samsung, the 7th Civil Chamber of Munich Regional Court has set out new approaches to FRAND proceedings. These deviate from the Higher Regional Court's position on one key point. Furthermore, the Regional Court found that the interim licence recently granted by the UK High Court is not decisive.

In a legal note following a “FRAND first” hearing, the Munich Regional Court’s 7th Civil Chamber laid out its approach to several questions not yet fully clarified by case law. The focus was on the significance of security deposits from patent users and the relevance of interim licences.

According to a spokesperson for Munich Regional Court, it remains unclear to what extent other chambers, particularly the 21st Civil Chamber which also handles patent law, will agree with the 7th Chamber’s assessments.

In November last year, Munich Higher Regional Court took the stance in HMD vs VoiceAge that “continuing willingness to take a licence” is met only if an implementer provides reasonable security, for example through a bank guarantee.

The Regional Court expressed doubts whether these principles would stand up in appeal proceedings at the Federal Court of Justice. The 7th Chamber takes a different view on what constitutes willingness.

According to the note, a licensee demonstrates willingness by quickly providing quantity information, having substantial own licensing relating to standards, being prepared to pay undisputed sums after sufficient negotiations, offering arbitration and providing security.
items.ai_summary_zh: 慕尼黑地方法院第七民事庭在三星与中兴的泛欧纠纷中，提出了 FRAND 诉讼的新方法。法院质疑上级法院关于“提供担保即代表许可意愿”的观点，认为英国高等法院颁发的临时许可非决定性因素。新标准要求被许可人快速提供数量信息、展示自身许可实践及准备支付无争议款项等综合行为来证明许可意愿。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院重新定义“许可意愿”，不再单一依赖银行担保作为判断标准", "明确英国高等法院授予的临时许可在德国 FRAND 程序中不具决定性", "强调被许可人需通过提供数据、仲裁意愿及付款准备等多维行动证明诚意"]
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应对 SEP 诉讼时，不能仅靠提供担保来规避禁令风险。应建立完善的许可谈判档案，主动披露实施数据并展现多维度的合作意愿，以适应德国法院新的审查标准，降低被判侵权的风险。
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["摒弃单一担保策略，构建包含数据披露与付款准备的综合抗辩体系", "重视德国本土司法倾向，勿过度依赖英国临时许可作为欧洲通用护身符"]

明确英国高等法院授予的临时许可在德国 FRAND 程序中不具决定性

items.title: SEP holders force UPC into FRAND dance with UK courts
items.title_zh: SEP 持有人迫使 UPC 介入并与英国法院展开费率管辖竞争
items.summary: Sun Patent has asked the UPC's Paris local division for a FRAND-rate determination in its dispute with Vivo.
items.content:
Firms & Experts Sponsored: Firm Profiles df-mp Patentanwälte Rechtsanwälte PartG mbB VOSSIUS & PARTNER Patentanwälte Rechtsanwälte mbB BOEHMERT & BOEHMERT View all Firm Profiles Sponsored: Expert Profiles Dr. Marcus Matuschek Nigel Stoate Dr Susanne Sonnenhauser View all Expert Profiles Sponsored Content UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP View all Sponsored Content

df-mp Patentanwälte Rechtsanwälte PartG mbB

VOSSIUS & PARTNER Patentanwälte Rechtsanwälte mbB

UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP

The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Sun Patent Trust has asked the UPC's Paris local division to set a FRAND rate in its dispute with Vivo. Ericsson has also filed a similar FRAND claim in its battle with Transsion. A FRAND-rate determination by the UPC could intensify competition with the UK High Court and Munich Regional Court.

Rumours have circulated among experts for some time. Since last Friday, it has become clear that the Unified Patent Court has received a complaint from an SEP holder regarding FRAND-rate determination.

At the end of November, the Court of Appeal rejected an application by implementer Vivo to halt the main proceedings in its dispute with Sun Patent Trust until the Court of Appeal decides whether the UPC has jurisdiction to make FRAND-rate determinations.

Sun Patent first applied to the UPC for a FRAND-rate determination in April. This forms part of two infringement actions brought by the Panasonic-affiliated licensing company against two SEPs at the Paris local division (case IDs: UPC_CFI_361/2025 and UPC_CFI_362/2025). Sun Patent aims to stop the sale of various 4G+ compatible Vivo devices.
items.ai_summary_zh: Sun Patent Trust 已向统一专利法院（UPC）巴黎本地分庭提起申请，请求裁定其与 Vivo 纠纷中的 FRAND 许可费率。此前爱立信也针对 Transsion 提出类似诉求。尽管英国上诉法院驳回 Vivo 暂停诉讼的申请，但 UPC 若确立费率裁决权，将与英国高等法院及慕尼黑地区法院形成直接管辖竞争，标志着欧洲 SEP 诉讼进入新阶段。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Sun Patent 请求 UPC 巴黎分庭裁定涉 Vivo 案件的 FRAND 许可…", "UPC 介入费率裁决将加剧与英国及德国法院的管辖权竞争", "英国上诉法院拒绝暂停主程序，等待 UPC 管辖权最终确认"]
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临更复杂的诉讼选择，需评估 UPC 与英德法院的策略差异。建议提前布局全球费率抗辩证据，关注 UPC 判例走向，避免因管辖权博弈陷入被动高额赔偿或禁售风险。
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕 UPC 成为 SEP 持有人新的择地行诉首选战场", "需统筹英、德、UPC 三地诉讼策略以优化全球和解筹码"]

items.title: Nokia vs Asus and Acer: Regional Court Munich redefines FRAND rules
items.title_zh: 诺基亚诉华硕宏碁：慕尼黑地方法院重塑 FRAND 规则
items.summary: Munich Regional Court ordered Asus and Acer to cease and desist in an infringement case concerning a Nokia video coding patent.
items.content:
Firms & Experts Sponsored: Firm Profiles Zimmermann & Partner Patentanwälte mbB KATHER AUGENSTEIN VOSSIUS & PARTNER Patentanwälte Rechtsanwälte mbB View all Firm Profiles Sponsored: Expert Profiles Wilbert Derks Alexander Schlotter Dr. Léon Dijkman View all Expert Profiles Sponsored Content UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP View all Sponsored Content

Zimmermann & Partner Patentanwälte mbB

VOSSIUS & PARTNER Patentanwälte Rechtsanwälte mbB

UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP

The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Last Thursday, Munich Regional Court ordered Asus and Acer to cease and desist in an infringement case concerning a Nokia video coding patent. The court delivered its judgment on the same day as the hearing. The court indicated further modifications to the Munich FRAND rules.

Just yesterday, the Federal Court of Justice upheld an injunction by the Regional and Higher Regional Court of Munich against HMD, largely confirming current Munich FRAND case law. Only days earlier, the Munich Regional Court heard another FRAND case. The dispute between Nokia and computer manufacturers Asus and Acer concerned the infringement of Nokia’s EP 2 375 749 for video coding.

The 7th Civil Chamber at Munich Regional Court, under presiding judge Oliver Schön, heard the two lawsuits for a full day on 22 January. On the same day the judges found that Acer and Asus are not willing to take a licence and had infringed EP 749 (case IDs: Acer 7 O 4100/25 and Asus 7 O 4102/25). It is considered certain that Nokia will enforce the judgments and that both implementers will appeal.

In spring 2025, Nokia filed patent claims against Acer, Asus and Hisense at Munich Regional Court and the UPC over video codec patents. Nokia alleged that Acer and Asus’ PCs and Hisense’s smart TVs infringe three of its patents. While proceedings concerning EP 2 375 749 and EP 2 774 375 are pending at Munich Regional Court, Nokia filed an action regarding EP 2 661 892 at the UPC local division Munich. All relate to the H.265 video codec standard.
items.ai_summary_zh: 慕尼黑地方法院就诺基亚视频编码专利侵权案，裁定华硕与宏碁停止侵权。法院认定被告不愿获得许可，同日宣判并进一步修正慕尼黑 FRAND 规则。此前联邦最高法院已确认类似禁令，诺基亚同时在统一专利法院提起相关诉讼，显示德国在 SEP 执法中的核心地位及规则趋严态势。

items.title: Federal Court of Justice hears FRAND appeal in VoiceAge vs HMD
items.title_zh: 德联邦最高法院审理 VoiceAge 诉 HMD 案中的 FRAND 上诉
items.summary: Today, the German Federal Court of Justice hears FRAND aspects in the appeal in VoiceAge vs HMD and will scrutinise Munich's FRAND approach.
items.content:
Firms & Experts Sponsored: Firm Profiles PAUSTIAN & PARTNER ARNOLD RUESS GOTTSCHALD View all Firm Profiles Sponsored: Expert Profiles Felix Hermann Harrison Chang, Ph.D. Sebastian Schneller View all Expert Profiles Sponsored Content UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP View all Sponsored Content

UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP

The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Today, the German Federal Court of Justice scrutinises Munich Higher Regional Court's new FRAND approach. The highest court's decision in the VoiceAge vs HMD case will have far-reaching consequences for European FRAND law. Once again, the EU Commission has intervened in the proceedings. JUVE Patent reporter Mathieu Klos is at the hearing.

After five years, FRAND is once again on the agenda in Karlsruhe. The city is home to the Federal Court of Justice, Germany’s highest civil court and final instance in patent proceedings. In 2020 and 2021, the court set out its interpretation of the CJEU’s European FRAND case law in Huawei vs ZTE with its two judgments Sisvel vs Haier I and II.

Today, the court will hear its next FRAND case in the appeal in VoiceAge vs HMD over EVS speech-coding patent EP 2 102 619 (case ID: KZR 10/25).

Previously, the Higher Regional Court Munich under presiding judge Lars Meinhardt found that HMD infringed VoiceAge’s EP 619 and is not a willing licensee (case ID: 6 U 3824/22 Kart).

The Regional Court Munich had also found HMD to be an unwilling licensee and ordered HMD to cease and desist (case ID: 7 O 14091/19 ).

HMD not only appealed to the Higher Regional Court Munich but also to the Federal Court of Justice in 2025. Given the fundamental importance of the FRAND-related issues, the Higher Regional Court Munich granted HMD leave to appeal to the Federal Court of Justice, but only concerning FRAND.
items.ai_summary_zh: 德国联邦最高法院今日开庭审理 VoiceAge 诉 HMD 案的 FRAND 上诉，重点审查慕尼黑高等法院的新裁判思路。该案涉及语音编码专利，此前下级法院认定 HMD 为非自愿被许可人。欧盟委员会再次介入，最高院判决将对欧洲 FRAND 法律产生深远影响，重申对华为诉中兴案的解释。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["德最高院审查慕尼黑法院关于非自愿被许可人的新认定标准", "欧盟委员会介入程序，凸显案件对欧洲竞争政策的重要性", "本案判决将重新界定欧洲标准必要专利许可的合规边界"]

items.title: Munich Regional Court further clarifies FRAND rules in Wilus vs Asus judgment
items.title_zh: 慕尼黑法院在 Wilus 诉华硕案中进一步澄清 FRAND 规则
items.summary: A recent judgment in Asus vs Wilus by Munich Regional Court is peppered with guiding principles for the Chamber's FRAND jurisprudence.
items.content:
Firms & Experts Sponsored: Firm Profiles WEICKMANN Simmons + Simmons LLP ARNOLD RUESS View all Firm Profiles Sponsored: Expert Profiles Prof. Dr. Heinz Goddar Chris Thornham Prof. Dr. Pablo Fernández Carballo-Calero View all Expert Profiles Sponsored Content UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP View all Sponsored Content

Prof. Dr. Pablo Fernández Carballo-Calero

UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP

The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

A partial payment obligation, comparative agreements for FRAND licences and FRAND calculation. A recent judgment by Munich Regional Court's 7th Civil Chamber is peppered with guiding principles for the chamber's FRAND jurisprudence. The judges ordered Asus to cease and desist due to the infringement of a Wilus wifi patent. In future, the chamber will not only look at the implementer's willingness to take a licence but also at the FRAND conformity of the SEP holder's offer.

Implementer Asus, SEP holder Wilus and their lawyers received a judgment yesterday that packs a punch. Munich Regional Court ordered Asus to cease and desist from infringing the Wifi 6 patent EP 3 512 289 . This much had been known since mid-January. Ever since, Asus has not been allowed to sell certain Wifi-6-compatible devices in Germany (case ID: Ref. 7 O 5007/25).

These include the Zenfone 11 Ultra smartphone, the ASUS Zenbook S 13, the ASUS Chromebox 4 mini PC and the ASUS AiO E3 (E3402) all-in-one PC. However, the plaintiff, the South Korean Wilus Institute of Standards and Technology, has not yet enforced the judgment. Asus can also appeal, which is considered certain.

The background to the lawsuit is the conclusion of a licence agreement for Sisvel’s Wifi 6 portfolio or bilaterally with the owner of the patent Wilus and SK Telecom. Last year, the South Korean R&D lab also launched a separate action against Asus at the Mannheim UPC local division alleging infringement of a second Wifi 6 patent.

Yesterday, the opposing parties and their lawyers received the written judgment with many fundamental comments, some of which went beyond the case. The 7th Civil Chamber has not had the opportunity to solidify its FRAND guideline for some time as many recent SEP cases have settled early. What the judges have now written into the Wilus vs Asus judgment is groundbreaking for future SEP proceedings at Munich Regional Court.
items.ai_summary_zh: 慕尼黑地区法院第七民事庭就 Wilus 诉华硕案作出判决，裁定华硕侵犯 WiFi 6 专利并须停止销售相关设备。该案核心意义在于法院确立了新的 FRAND  jurisprudence 指导原则：未来不仅审查实施者的许可意愿，还将严格评估 SEP 持有人的报价是否符合 FRAND 原则。尽管原告尚未执行禁令且被告预计上诉，但该判决为德国后续 SEP 诉讼奠定了重要基准。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院裁定华硕侵权并禁止其在德国销售特定 WiFi 6 兼容设备", "确立新规则：将同时审查实施者意愿与权利人报价的 FRAND 合规性", "此案填补了慕尼黑法院近期因和解频发而缺乏的 FRAND 判例空白"]
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临更平衡的 SEP 诉讼环境，需重视双向合规。建议出口企业不仅要证明自身许可意愿，更要建立对权利人报价进行 FRAND 合规性抗辩的能力，避免因单方让步陷入被动，同时关注上诉进程以调整市场策略。
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["从单方审查转向双向评估，中国企业需提升报价合规抗辩能力", "面对禁令风险，应结合上诉策略与市场库存管理制定综合应对方案"]

items.title: Local division Mannheim holds single day hearing for ZTE vs Samsung
items.title_zh: UPC 曼海姆分部单日审理中兴诉三星侵权及 FRAND 抗辩
items.summary: The local division Mannheim will hear ZTE's infringement claim and Samsung's FRAND defence in the dispute over 4G and 5G.
items.content:
Firms & Experts Sponsored: Firm Profiles BARDEHLE PAGENBERG Taylor Wessing PENTARC Rechtsanwälte PartG mbB View all Firm Profiles Sponsored: Expert Profiles Daniel Wise Dr. rer. nat., Dipl.-Phys., Dipl.-Kfm. Georg Anetsberger Tom Foster View all Expert Profiles Sponsored Content UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP View all Sponsored Content

Dr. rer. nat., Dipl.-Phys., Dipl.-Kfm. Georg Anetsberger

UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP

The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

The local division Mannheim had originally scheduled the hearing for three days. It has since been reduced to one. With no settlement in sight, the local division will hear ZTE's infringement claim and Samsung's FRAND defence in the dispute over global 4G and 5G licences over the next few hours.

The global SEP dispute with ZTE has not gone well for Samsung so far. The South Korean mobile phone giant has not managed to prevail with an application for an interim licence in the UK. Furthermore, Frankfurt Regional Court recently dismissed an antitrust action against ZTE. And now, according to JUVE Patent sources, Samsung has likely received an unfavourable preliminary opinion from the European Patent Office’s Opposition Division regarding the validity of EP 4 096 288 .

The patent was the basis for one of Samsung’s lawsuits at the UPC in the global dispute with ZTE over a global licence for 4G and 5G. The local division Mannheim under presiding judge Peter Tochtermann has now provisionally suspended the proceedings until the validity of EP 288 has been clarified (case ID: UPC_CFI_188/2025 ).

The local division had planned a double hearing for today and tomorrow. The programme initially included one infringement claim from ZTE and one from Samsung. Following the latest developments, the Mannheim local division will now only hear ZTE’s infringement action regarding EP 3 905 730 (case ID: UPC_CFI_850/2024 ).

The patent protects a system information transmission method and device. In addition to the infringement of EP 730, the question of whether both parties have behaved in a FRAND-compliant manner with their offers is also on today’s agenda. This is common practice in major SEP battles. In addition, Samsung filed a FRAND counterclaim in late 2025, which is also on today’s agenda.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院曼海姆地方分院将单日审理中兴对三星的 4G/5G 专利侵权主张及三星的 FRAND 抗辩。因三星涉案专利在 EPO 异议部获不利初步意见，法院已中止相关程序。此前三星在英国申请临时许可失败且在德国反垄断诉讼受挫，此次听证聚焦中兴专利有效性及双方 FRAND 合规性。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["曼海姆分院缩减庭审为一天，专注中兴侵权案与三星 FRAND 抗辩", "三星涉案专利遭 EPO 不利初步意见，导致部分诉讼程序被暂时中止", "三星在全球 SEP 纠纷中接连受挫，包括英国许可申请被拒及德国反垄断败诉"]
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应对 SEP 诉讼需高度重视 EPO 异议程序对 UPC 侵权诉讼的阻断效应。建议提前布局专利稳定性分析，并在 FRAND 谈判中保留完整证据链，以应对欧盟法院日益严格的合规审查。

brief.headline_zh: 欧盟执法升级与确权新策
brief.headline_en: EU Enforcement Surge and New Registration Tactics
brief.summary_zh: 本期 EUIPO 动态显示欧盟正全面升级知识产权执法，重点打击假药及有组织犯罪，并推广数字工具。司法层面明确功能性形状不可注册商标，但认可“单点实体 + 线上”模式构成真实使用。此外，手工艺地理标志新体系落地，中小企业可利用公益法律支持降低维权成本。
key_points: EUIPO 联合 Europol 等机构升级全欧打假行动，重点针对假药及有组织犯罪网络。
司法判例确立纯功能性形状无法注册商标，但单一地点服务结合线上可证成真实使用。
手工艺地理标志新体系正式运行，EUIPO 扩大 SMEs 公益法律支持项目覆盖范围。
actions: 排查出口产品是否涉及纯功能性形状设计，避免盲目申请商标导致驳回。
利用“线下单点 + 线上销售”模式积累证据，以低成本维持欧盟商标有效性。
评估中国传统手工艺品申请欧盟地理标志保护的可行性，提前布局国家初审。
watchlist: IPEP 数字执法工具在欧盟海关的实操扣货效率变化。
手工艺地理标志双阶段审查程序中出现的异议案例趋势。
欧盟针对有组织犯罪知识产权案件的刑事判决力度。

items.title: EUIPO supports major Europe-wide action against counterfeit medicines
items.title_zh: EUIPO 支持全欧打击假药重大行动
items.summary: EUIPO supports major Europe-wide action against counterfeit medicines
items.content: EUIPO supports major Europe-wide action against counterfeit medicines EUR 32 million worth of fake medicines and illicit doping substances have been seized across Europe in Operation SHIELD VI, a major operation led by Europol with the support of the European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO), alongside other international partners. Carried out between April and November 2025, the operation brought together authorities from 30 European countries. It led to nearly 500 arrests, more than 3,300 prosecutions and investigations into over 40 organised crime groups. In addition, 66 websites used to sell counterfeit medicines were shut down. The seized products included over 3 million fake pills and nearly 300 000 illegal packages of doping substances, including Semaglutide-based products marketed as fat-burners. Some of these counterfeit medicines closely resemble legitimate products, including opioids, which significantly increases the risk of poisoning and overdose. Through its support to Operation SHIELD VI, the EUIPO contributed its expertise in tackling intellectual property crime and analysing online infringement trends linked to counterfeit pharmaceuticals, reinforcing coordinated action to protect consumers and legitimate businesses across the EU. A growing threat for European consumers According to the latest data, trade in counterfeit and illicit medicines is on the rise, coming at a high cost not only for individual consumers but also for society as a whole. Beyond infringing intellectual property rights, these products pose a serious threat to public health, as they often contain dangerous, substandard or unknown substances. The misuse of prescription and over-the-counter medicines, together with the growth of unregulated websites selling fake medicines, continues to fuel illegal markets and further complicates enforcement efforts by making detection and interception more difficult. To better protect consumers from counterfeit medicines and promote safer online purchases, the EUIPO, together with the European Medicines Agency (EMA) and Europol, launched the campaign “Stay on top – EU vs Fake Medicines”. The initiative raises awareness about the risks of buying medicines from unverified online sources and provides guidance on how to identify authorised sellers.

items.title: IPEP Presented in Athens: Strengthening IP Enforcement Through Digital Tools
items.title_zh: EUIPO 于雅典展示 IPEP：以数字工具强化知识产权保护
items.summary: IPEP Presented in Athens: Strengthening IP Enforcement Through Digital Tools
items.content: IPEP Presented in Athens: Strengthening IP Enforcement Through Digital Tools The EUIPO´s IP Enforcement Portal (IPEP) was presented during the 1st edition of the ‘training for officers on the field’ co‑organised by the Greek Independent Authority for Market Control and Consumer’s Protection and the Global Anti-Counterfeiting Group (GACG), represented by INDICAM. The event was supported by the EUIPO, and took place last week, 27 February 2026 in Athens, Greece. It gathered representatives of all Greek competent authorities that detect and seize counterfeit such as customs, municipal police and the authority for market control. The training addressed officers working on the field, being on the front line to protect the Greek market from counterfeit items. It brought together right holders and national authorities to exchange practical information on the detection of fakes. About the event The one-day hands-on training was co‑organised by the Greek Independent Authority for Market Control and Consumer’s Protection and the Global Anti-Counterfeiting Group, represented by INDICAM, with support from the EUIPO. The training aimed to support frontline law enforcement officers in targeting and detecting counterfeit goods. It provided practical information on frequently counterfeited products such as automobile spare parts, fashion apparel, and tobacco products presented by the right holders themselves. Around 15 companies presented their IP portfolios, key infringement challenges, and technical guidance for identifying counterfeit items. IPEP: A unified digital tool for enforcement authorities At the event, the EUIPO team showcased how IPEP supports Greek and EU enforcement officers by enabling secure, real‑time information exchange between authorities and rights holders. IPEP Mobile App The team also presented the mobile version of IPEP, offering field‑ready access to: Multilingual, real‑time search to IP rights, product and contact point information on the spot. suspicious‑case (detention notification) creation and syncing— all optimised for rapid use by Greek enforcement officers. Moving Forward The seminar reaffirmed the commitment of the Greek national authorities, industry stakeholders represented by the GACG and the EUIPO to deepen cooperation and expand the use of digital enforcement tools like IPEP across Greece. Additional training sessions and onboarding support will continue throughout 2026–2027.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）在希腊雅典联合当地市场监管机构及全球反假冒组织，举办针对一线执法人员的培训活动。会议重点展示了知识产权执法门户（IPEP）及其移动应用，旨在通过数字化手段实现权利人与执法部门间的实时信息交换，提升对汽车零件、时尚及烟草等假冒商品的查获效率，并计划于 2026 至 2027 年深化在希腊的合作与推广。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["EUIPO 向希腊一线执法人员展示 IPEP 数字执法工具", "培训涵盖假冒商品识别技术及权利人实务经验分享", "双方承诺深化合作，将在 2026 至 2027 年持续推广该工具"]
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出口企业应关注欧盟数字化执法趋势，主动利用 IPEP 平台备案知识产权。建议企业配合当地执法部门提供真伪鉴别技术支持，建立快速响应机制，以降低产品在希腊及欧盟市场的被侵权风险。
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用 IPEP 平台备案信息，提升欧盟海关扣货效率", "加强政企协作，为希腊执法提供产品鉴别技术支持"]

items.title: EUIPO holds first Plenary Meeting of the Advisory Board for Craft and Industrial Geographical Indications
items.title_zh: EUIPO 召开手工艺与工业地理标志咨询委员会首次全会
items.summary: EUIPO holds first Plenary Meeting of the Advisory Board for Craft and Industrial Geographical Indications
items.content: EUIPO holds first Plenary Meeting of the Advisory Board for Craft and Industrial Geographical Indications Today, the European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) held the first plenary meeting of the Advisory Board for Craft and Industrial Geographical Indications (CIGIs) marking an important milestone in the implementation of the new EU framework for the protection of craft and industrial geographical indications. The meeting provided an initial platform for exchange on key horizontal and organisational issues linked to the implementation of the CIGI system aiming at ensuring an efficient, coherent and consistent application of the new Regulation across the European Union. Supporting EUIPO’s new competence The Advisory Board plays a central role in supporting EUIPO’s newly acquired competence in the field of craft and industrial geographical indications. Its mandate is threefold: to provide general opinions on the assessment of quality criteria, the establishment of a geographical indication’s reputation, the determination of the generic nature of names and the assessment of potential risks of consumer confusion. to offer expert opinions on specific geographical indication applications when requested by EUIPO’s Geographical Indications Division, the Boards of Appeal or the European Commission. and to act as a forum for cooperation between Member States, the Commission and the Office, facilitating the exchange of best practices and promoting the efficiency of national procedures. Key topics and outcomes During its first plenary meeting the Advisory Board elected its Chairperson and Deputy Chairperson, formally enabling the body to begin operating under the Regulation and its Rules of Procedure. Also, members discussed the mandate and working methods of the Advisory Board, received updates on the implementation of the Craft and Industrial Geographical Indications Regulation from EUIPO and the European Commission and exchanged views on progress at national level in the Member States. The meeting also included an initial discussion on the Guidelines for Geographical Indications for craft and industrial products, which will support consistent application of the new system. Next steps Following this inaugural meeting, the Advisory Board will become fully operational and ready to exercise its functions in line with the Geographical Indications Regulation and its Rules of Procedure. EUIPO’s Geographical Indications Division, and where applicable the Boards of Appeal, may consult the Advisory Board at any stage of the procedures for registration, opposition, appeal, amendment of product specifications or cancellation of registrations. The establishment and activation of the Advisory Board represent a significant step forward in the roll-out of the EU system for the protection of craft and industrial geographical indications, reinforcing cooperation among EU institutions and Member States and contributing to the effective protection of Europe’s cultural and industrial heritage.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）召开手工艺与工业地理标志（CIGIs）咨询委员会首次全会，标志欧盟该领域保护框架正式实施。会议选举了领导层，明确了委员会在质量评估、声誉认定及防止名称通用化方面的咨询职能，并讨论了实施指南。此举旨在确保新条例在全欧范围内高效、一致地适用，加强成员国与欧盟机构间的协作。

items.title: Amplifying the fight against organised crime: EMPACT Communicators Network at EUIPO
items.title_zh: 强化打击有组织犯罪：EMPACT 传播者网络会议在欧盟知识产权局召开
items.summary: Amplifying the fight against organised crime: EMPACT Communicators Network at EUIPO
items.content: Amplifying the fight against organised crime: EMPACT Communicators Network at EUIPO The European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) hosted the European Multidisciplinary Platform Against Criminal Threats (EMPACT) Communicators Network in Alicante, bringing together communication experts from across the European Union to strengthen how joint action against serious and organised crime is explained to the public. Participants highlighted the importance of effective communication, as EMPACT enters a new operational cycle (2026–2029). Among the objectives: sustain political momentum, support operational excellence, reinforce public confidence and raise awareness of EU action against serious and organised international crime. Discussions focused as well on the need of strengthening the visibility of EMPACT, as a cornerstone of EU law enforcement cooperation. Cooperation at all levels EMPACT is the EU’s flagship framework for cooperation against serious and organised crime. It brings together EU Member States, EU agencies and international partners to identify common priorities and deliver coordinated investigations and operational actions targeting the most significant criminal threats facing Europe. Through EMPACT, law enforcement authorities across Europe work together to dismantle criminal networks involved in areas such as trafficking, fraud, cybercrime and intellectual property crime. These joint efforts translate EU priorities into concrete operational results on the ground. Protecting citizens, businesses and the internal market. Making this impact visible is essential. Clear and strategic communication helps to show how EU cooperation works, strengthens trust among institutions and citizens, and ensures that operational achievements are understood beyond specialist audiences. The EMPACT Communicators Network plays a key role in aligning messages, sharing best practices and amplifying results across Europe. The European Union Intellectual Property Office is hosting this year’s EMPACT Communicators Network meeting to help strengthen cooperation among network members.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局在阿利坎特主办 EMPACT 传播者网络会议，汇聚欧盟各国沟通专家，旨在加强针对严重有组织犯罪的联合行动宣传。会议聚焦提升 EMPACT 作为欧盟执法合作基石的可见度，涵盖打击贩运、欺诈及知识产权犯罪等领域，以巩固公众信心并支持 2026-2029 新运营周期。

items.title: Intellectual Property for all: The critical importance of IP rights to SMEs
items.title_zh: 知识产权惠及全员：IP 权利对中小企业的至关重要性
items.summary: Intellectual Property for all: The critical importance of IP rights to SMEs
items.content: Intellectual Property for all: The critical importance of IP rights to SMEs By Eleonora Rosati, Professor of IP Law, Stockholm University, LinkedIn Small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) represent 99.8% of all businesses in the European Union (EU) and intellectual property (IP) is central to their commercial success and also to the overall competitiveness of the EU. Whether you are launching a new product, opening a fashion boutique featuring local brands, or building a tech start-up, IP rights (IPRs) can make a difference as a strategic asset which can help you unlock new opportunities and safeguard the value of your ingenuity. Protecting intangible assets – including for example your name, logo, products, services, and creative outputs – is essential to positioning your business for long-term success. Why we need to talk about the importance of IP for SMEs The strategic importance of IP for the EU´s economy is undeniable. As it is outlined in a recent joint study of the European Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) and the European Patent Office (EPO), companies that hold IP rights perform better than those that do not: they generate 23.8% more revenue per employee than companies without IPRs. When adjusting for factors like industry, company size, and location, this revenue advantage increases to 41%, with an even greater impact observed among SMEs. When a SME registers its IPRs, it achieves in general 44% higher revenue per employee compared to their counterparts without such protection. When we speak about SMEs focused on innovation – including tech startups – another joint EUIPO/EPO study indicates that IPR‑intensive industries attract the overwhelming majority of private equity and venture capital investment in the EU. Over 88% of total funding, equivalent to €70.7 billion, was invested between 2021 and 2023 in startups operating in IPR‑intensive industries, underscoring the importance of IP in fostering innovation and growth. Furthermore, it is reported that IPR-intensive industries pay significantly higher wages than other industries, with a wage premium of 40.9%. These numbers are in line with those reported in the European Commission´s recently published 2025 Annual Single Market and Competitiveness Report. While also showcasing the critical role of IP to commercial success and as a signal of firms’ innovativeness, the report indicates that startups are ten times more likely to obtain early-stage venture capital if they have registered patents or trade marks. Also, IPRs are associated with a 200% higher likelihood of successful exit. It is thus clear that IP plays a key role, and so is obtaining the relevant protections under IP law. But have you ever thought of what role different IPRs play in helping your SME or startup thrive? Different IPRs to protect different intangible assets Starting with your name and logo, these can be protected as trade marks. If you, for example, register a trade mark with the EUIPO, you will receive protection for the entire EU territory. A key requirement is that your name or logo is sufficiently distinctive, by that meaning that the sign in question will be perceived by consumers of the relevant goods and/or services as a badge of commercial origin. Furthermore, if you operate a website, the look-and-feel of your website and its domain name will be also protected by IP. The appearance of your products may be safeguarded through design rights or copyright. Registered designs offer broader and longer-lasting protection than unregistered ones, which arise automatically. If, for example, you want to register your design and receive protection across the entire EU territory, once again you will need to apply to the EUIPO. Copyright also arises automatically and requires originality, that is: the result of free and creative choices. This means that, if your product’s appearance is dictated purely by technical considerations, copyright protection will not apply. If your product or service includes an invention – for example, a novel technology in a hair dryer or an innovative fabric in your garments – you may seek patent protection. And do not forget that confidential business information – such as methods, recipes, or formulas – can be protected as trade secrets. How the EU and the EUIPO support SMEs with their IP Considering the numbers and the role that different IPRs play, it is clear that getting the IP ‘right’ should be a priority for any company, not only for SMEs. It is for all these reasons that the Ideas Powered for business SME Fund has been devised by the European Commission and implemented by the EUIPO. This is a grant scheme designed to help EU-based SMEs protect their IPRs. The new edition was launched on 2 February and will run until 4 December 2026. SMEs can also receive advice in their preferred language from experts across Europe on how to protect their IPRs, as well as what to do in case someone claims that their own rights are being infringed. Of course, all of this is also consistent with the broader long-term strategy and priorities of the EUIPO, including awareness raising, promotion, and enforcement of IPRs. If one for example reviews the EUIPO’s 2030 Strategic Plan, Goal 3 is all about improving access to the IP system and promote the use of IPRs in support of innovation-driven economic growth. This can be also achieved by helping the next generation of innovators to thrive, creating innovative products and services, promoting innovation, value creation and inclusion. In sum: If you want your business or start-up to thrive tomorrow, start thinking about your IP strategy today!
items.ai_summary_zh: 斯德哥尔摩大学教授撰文指出，知识产权是欧盟中小企业商业成功的关键。EUIPO 与 EPO 联合研究显示，拥有 IP 权利的中小企业人均营收高出 44%，且更易获得风险投资。报告强调商标与专利等无形资产保护能显著提升企业估值、工资水平及退出成功率，是创新密集型产业获取资金的核心信号。

items.title: New EU System for Geographical Indications for Craft and Industrial Products – important reminder for applicants
items.title_zh: 欧盟手工业产品地理标志新体系申请重要提醒
items.summary: New EU System for Geographical Indications for Craft and Industrial Products – important reminder for applicants
items.content: New EU System for Geographical Indications for Craft and Industrial Products – important reminder for applicants As of 1 December 2025, the Office has assumed competence for registering applications for the new intellectual property right covering Geographical Indications for Craft and Industrial Products (CIGIs). By 2 December 2026, all existing national protection systems will be phased out and replaced by the new EU system. Under the standard procedure, EU producer groups and other applicants from twenty EU Member States can submit applications for the protection of new CIGIs to their respective national competent authorities. These authorities will conduct the national phase examination, which includes examining the application and launching the national opposition procedure. Once the national examination phase is successfully completed, the competent authorities will forward the application to the Office for the examination at Union phase to take place. This phase involves verifying the absence of manifest errors, initiating the EU-level opposition procedure, and ultimately registering the CIGI in the Union register. Applicants from remaining seven EU Member States - from Denmark, Finland, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, the Netherlands and Sweden will file applications directly with the EUIPO. In addition, EU Member States may seek protection for any existing CIGIs protected under national law or established by use prior to 1 December 2025. To facilitate the process, the Office has developed GIportal, an intuitive e-filing tool for CIGIs. The first weeks of implementation have already demonstrated strong interest in the scheme. In fact, until 27 February 2026, the Office published 64 applications for existing names submitted by France, Portugal, Slovakia and Sweden primarily in the sectors of stones and minerals, textiles, and ceramics. Webinars, workshops, detailed FAQs as well as countless other sources of useful information can be found on the EUIPO’s GI Hub.
items.ai_summary_zh: 自 2025 年 12 月 1 日起，EUIPO 正式负责注册手工业和工业产品地理标志（CIGIs）。至 2026 年 12 月，各国原有制度将全面被欧盟新体系取代。多数成员国申请人需经国家阶段审查后转交 EUIPO，而丹麦等七国可直接向 EUIPO 申请。目前已有多国提交现有名称保护申请，EUIPO 推出 GIportal 工具并配套指南以协助过渡。

items.ai_insight_zh: 中国涉欧手工艺品及工业品出口企业应关注新体系过渡期，评估现有产品是否符合 CIGI 保护标准。建议利用 EUIPO 新平台提前布局，通过欧盟统一注册降低多国维权成本，提升品牌在欧洲市场的法律保护层级。

items.title: Pro Bono Legal Support for SMEs: Call for Design Rights Experts as demand grows
items.title_zh: EUIPO 呼吁外观设计专家加入 SMEs 公益法律支持项目
items.summary: Pro Bono Legal Support for SMEs: Call for Design Rights Experts as demand grows
items.content: Pro Bono Legal Support for SMEs: Call for Design Rights Experts as demand grows The EUIPO Pro Bono Programme continues to expand, confirming the importance of early-stage IP support for European SMEs, particularly microenterprises, without professional representation. In 2025, the programme received 1,375 SME support requests, the highest number since its launch and part of a steady annual increase since 2020. Over 90% of applicants were microenterprises, and requests came from all EU Member States, with strongest participation from Germany, Spain, Italy, France and the Netherlands, alongside growing engagement from smaller countries. The EUIPO extends its sincere thanks to all the IP practitioners who give generously of their time to provide the pro bono service. What SMEs request Demand remains strongest for trade mark-related support, but requests also cover design protection, disputes and documentation: 49% of SMEs needed help preparing an IP application 23% sought guidance on selecting the most appropriate IP rights SMEs report very high satisfaction, with 9 out of 10 recommending the programme, citing the speed, quality and professionalism of the expert support. IP Experts: A Growing Network The Pro Bono network now includes 289 active IP experts, with 165 new members in 2025, and strong representation in Italy, Germany and Spain. Support spans all major IP fields, including trade marks and designs, with most consultations lasting 1- 4hours, ensuring sustainability for practitioners. Experts are largely satisfied with their participation, and some pro bono interactions have evolved into long-term professional relationships. Focus for 2026: Design Protection In 2026, the programme will place a stronger emphasis on engaging designers and design-driven SMEs, reinforcing the need for IP representatives with expertise in design rights. Outreach will be expanded, the expert network further strengthened, and user experience improved for both SMEs and professionals. IP practitioners with experience in design protection are particularly encouraged to join the EUIPO Pro Bono Expert Network and support Europe’s creative and innovative businesses at a critical stage of their development. To work as a Pro Bono Expert, sign up here
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）公益项目需求激增，2025 年受理请求创历史新高，超九成为微型企业。尽管商标咨询仍占主导，但 2026 年将战略重心转向外观设计保护，急需相关领域专家加入网络。该项目覆盖全欧盟，旨在为缺乏专业代表的初创企业提供高质量知识产权指导，用户满意度极高。
