news_items.title: UPC Munich Local Division rejects GXD-Bio’s claim against Myriad; EP 3 346 403 revoked
news_items.title_zh: UPC 慕尼黑驳回 GXD-Bio 诉请并撤销 Myriad 涉案专利
news_items.summary: Summary/Background The Local Division (LD) Munich dismissed GDX-Bio’s infringement action against My
news_items.content: The Local Division (LD) Munich dismissed GDX-Bio’s infringement action against Myriad and revoked EP 3 346 403 for added matter. EP 3 346 403 claimed a method for quantifying expression level of a target gene in FFPE breast cancer tissue comprising normalizing an expression level of the target gene to that of the endogenous reference gene OAZ1. Myriad was represented by HOFFMANN EITLE ( Joachim Renken , Niels Hölder, Michael Pfeifer , and Claudia Unsin ). The LD revoked EP 3 346 403 as it contained added matter. The application to amend the patent based on Auxiliary Requests 1, 2, or 3, filed by GXD-Bio in response to Myriad’s counterclaim for revocation, was refused for the same reasons. In its assessment of added matter, the LD applied what is referred to as the “ gold standard ” for assessing added matter, as laid out by the Court of Appeal 1 , and provided its concrete, reasoned findings. Original claim 25 disclosed a method for quantifying an expression level of a target gene comprising three steps but did not mention that the target gene is in an FFPE tissue sample of human breast cancer. The LD found that this feature was not disclosed as belonging to the invention . The objective of the invention was instead to identify universal reference genes that are expressed in many different human tissues and are not limited to their use on specific tissue samples or in specific studies. For the LD, it was clear the person skilled in the art will set out to read the application with this background in mind. 2 GXD-Bio relied on Experimental Example 7 as further alleged basis for claim 1. While Experimental Example 7 disclosed expression stability (which is an important property for a reference gene) of 12 novel reference genes for a pool of different FFPE tissue samples, including breast cancer FFPE samples, it did not provide any information on expression stability in the specific FFPE tissues (such as breast cancer tissue). With this understanding, the skilled person would merely derive from this disclosure that the novel reference genes could be applied to FFPE samples in general. The LD followed Myriad’s argument that Experimental Example 7 is not an embodiment of original claim 25 3 and ruled that “ it is not sufficient that breast cancer tissue samples are disclosed. Rather, they must also be disclosed as belonging directly and unambiguously to the invention. However, this cannot be established as the application teaches the person skilled in the art that the technical problem is to find universal genes which are not limited to use on specific tissue samples .” 4 The LD’s consideration of a “ disclosure as belonging to the invention ” in the context of added matter is somewhat reminiscent of the German case law, e.g. the Federal Court of Justice (BGH) decision X ZR 75/08 – Reifenabdichtmittel. 5 In this decision, the BGH found that the disclosure of a product containing certain components does not equally disclose a product consisting of these components as belonging to the invention in the absence of any indication in the original application text that it is particularly advantageous or otherwise desired that the product exclusively consists of these components. 6 The LD further held that “[ t ] here is no pointer in the application … that the FFPE breast cancer tissue samples are particularly preferred ”. 7 T he lack of a pointer referred to by the LD is a criterion well-established in the case law of the EPO’s Boards of Appeal: a combination of features taken from separate embodiments of the original application is considered to constitute added matter in the absence of a pointer to that particular combination. 8 Claim 1 further required that OAZ1 is used as a reference gene in the method for quantifying an expression level of a target gene. The LD found that in Experimental Example 7, OAZ1 was (reliably) detected in all 60 FFPE samples, including the 10 FFPE breast cancer tissues samples. Experimental Example 7 was nevertheless found to lack a disclosure of OAZ1 as the (sole) reference gene for normalizing the expression level of a target gene in a FFPE breast cancer sample: OAZ1 was neither described as the best reference gene for FFPE tissue samples nor did the data reveal the expression stability of OAZ1 in FFPE breast cancer tissue samples. 9 Considering the original application in its entirety, the LD concluded that OAZ1 was not singled out as the reference gene in the claimed method, neither for normalizing the expression level of a target gene, nor with regard to FFPE tissue samples. 10 Whereas the LD refers to the singling out from the original application, in the terminology applied by the EPO’s Boards of Appeal, the expression “ singling out ” usually refers to a claimed feature. In essence, however, it reveals the same underlying concept, namely that singling out a feature in the claim without direct and unambiguous basis in the original application results in added matter, in line with the case law of the EPO’s Enlarged Board of Appeal. 11 Using EPO terminology, one could say that the combination of OAZ1 with FFPE breast cancer sample tissues was singled out from different lists, a list of reference genes and a list of FFPE tissue samples, thereby creating new subject matter in line with the EPO’s “ two-list principle ”. GXD-Bio’s infringement action against Myriad et al. was dismissed by the LD. Myriad’s EndoPredict test involves measuring of expression levels of eight target genes and three reference genes, i.e. OAZ1, CALM2, and RPL37A. The relative expression of each of the eight target genes is determined by normalization to an average expression level of OAZ1, CALM2, and RPL37A. The LD construed claim 1 of EP 3 346 403 as requiring normalization to a single reference gene, i.e., to OAZ1 only. In the contested EndoPredict assay, there is only one normalization calculation to the average value of the expression levels of the three reference genes, which is not ident
news_items.ai_summary_zh: UPC 慕尼黑地方法院驳回 GXD-Bio 对 Myriad 的侵权诉讼，并以“新增事项”为由撤销 EP3346403 号专利。法院适用“黄金标准”，认定原申请未明确将乳腺癌 FFPE 组织样本纳入发明范畴，实验数据仅示通用性而非特定归属，故辅助请求亦被拒。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧申请专利时，需确保权利要求特征在原说明书中有直接明确依据，避免依赖实施例推导特定应用场景。面对 UPC 诉讼，应重视新增事项抗辩策略，提前排查文件披露充分性风险。

news_items.title: The Dracula paradox: temporal limitations in EU trademark law
news_items.title_zh: 德古拉悖论：欧盟商标法中的时间限制难题
news_items.summary: The CJEU's recent judgment in Lunapark (C-452/24) has sent shockwaves through EU trademark law by holding that national courts cannot limit trademark rights for reasons of tolerance beyond the concept of acquiescence provided in Articles 18(1) and 9(1)-(2) of Directive 2015/2436.
news_items.content: The Dracula paradox: temporal limitations in EU trademark law The CJEU's recent judgment in Lunapark ( C-452/24 ) has sent shockwaves through EU trademark law by holding that national courts cannot limit trademark rights for reasons of tolerance beyond the concept of acquiescence provided in Articles 18(1) and 9(1)-(2) of Directive 2015/2436. The case involved a dispute where Lunapark held the registered "DRACULA" trademark whilst Hardeco's predecessor had used the sign for years without any exclusive rights. Despite Lunapark's significant delay in bringing proceedings, the Finnish court's application of a "reasonable time" requirement was challenged. The CJEU held that Article 18(1) fully harmonises the conditions for limiting a trademark's exclusive rights due to holder inactivity, allowing for acquiescence of a subsequently registered trademark, but not an unregistered sign which confers no exclusive right. The judgment is grounded in textual interpretation: if Article 18(1) expressly mentions acquiescence to later registered trademarks, this excludes tolerance of unregistered signs. The CJEU correctly observed that neither Karkkimies nor Hardeco acquired exclusive rights over "Dracula" through registration or use, protecting the integrity of the trademark registration system. The CJEU prevented Member States from circumventing harmonised acquiescence rules through general principles of national law, ensuring uniform application across the EU. Despite its doctrinal coherence, the judgment raises serious concerns. Most significantly, the CJEU fails to adequately distinguish between substantive limitations and procedural rules governing enforcement. The Finnish "reasonable time" rule arguably functioned as a procedural requirement, and the CJEU should have assessed it against the principles of equivalence and effectiveness rather than excluding its application outright. In areas of full harmonisation, Member States retain procedural autonomy only to the extent that their rules do not undermine uniform application of EU law. The CJEU's own jurisprudence in Rewe-Zentralfinanz ( C-33/76 ) and Comet ( C-45/76 ) established that national limitation periods are compatible with EU law provided they satisfy the equivalence and effectiveness tests. A crucial distinction lies in available remedies: many Member States time-bar damage claims differently from injunctive relief, and clarifying that national time limits may bar damage claims but not injunctions would reduce conflict with EU law whilst maintaining uniform trademark protection. The judgment gives insufficient weight to legitimate expectations of third parties who have used signs for extended periods without opposition, potentially producing inequitable results where a party using a sign for 20 years faces infringement proceedings despite the trademark owner's prolonged inaction. This tension between legal certainty and equitable outcomes lies at the heart of the Lunapark paradox. Moreover, the practical implications extend beyond individual disputes. Businesses operating across multiple Member States now face uncertainty regarding how long they might remain vulnerable to infringement claims. The elimination of national temporal limitations could encourage strategic delay by trademark proprietors, undermining the policy objectives of promoting diligent enforcement and protecting reliance interests. On 3 November 2025, the Municipal Court in Prague referred three critical questions to the CJEU in MPM v ELTON ( C-693/25 ), directly challenging the consequences of Lunapark . The first question asks whether the acquiescence period may be suspended during legal impossibility. The second asks whether the right to prohibit use can become time-barred under national law before the five-year EU period expires. This is a direct test of the harmonisation-autonomy balance. The third concerns prior use rights where a sign was used for over 50 years before the later trademark registration. The central issue is whether full harmonisation of substantive rights precludes all national temporal limitations. Whilst national rules creating additional substantive grounds for limiting trademark rights are incompatible with Directive 2015/2436, procedural rules governing enforcement should remain within national competence subject to equivalence and effectiveness. The CJEU should clarify in MPM v ELTON that national procedural rules governing enforcement timing remain within national competence. The EU legislature should also consider amending Directive 2015/2436 to explicitly preserve Member States' procedural autonomy whilst addressing the relationship between acquiescence and limitation periods, suspension during legal impossibility, and substantive prior use rights. The fundamental question remains: should mere registration always triumph over decades of good-faith use? The answer may determine whether EU trademark law achieves its goal of balancing proprietor rights with legitimate expectations of third parties, or whether it creates a system where rights exist indefinitely without meaningful temporal boundaries on enforcement. ©2025 Kluwer Law International, part of Wolters Kluwer. news_items.title: Neurim in the mirror - Carpmaels & Ransford - Law Firm
news_items.title_zh: 镜像中的 Neurim：德国法院就 SPC 首份授权定义提请欧盟法院裁决
news_items.summary: There has been a new reference made to the CJEU on the correct interpretation of Article 3(d) of the SPC Regulation (Regulation (EC) No 469/2009), this time from the Bundespatentgericht (BPatG), the German Federal Patent Court. The reference concerns what counts as a “first authorisation” to place a product on the market. The CJEU has […]
news_items.content: There has been a new reference made to the CJEU on the correct interpretation of Article 3(d) of the SPC Regulation (Regulation (EC) No 469/2009), this time from the Bundespatentgericht (BPatG), the German Federal Patent Court. The reference concerns what counts as a “first authorisation” to place a product on the market. The CJEU has flip-flopped on this point for many years: taking a strict approach in Pharmacia (C-31/03) by denying an SPC for an active ingredient previously authorised for a different use, then reversing this in its landmark decision in Neurim (C-130/11) which opened the doors to SPCs for new uses of known active ingredients, but then most recently reverting back to its previous, strict case law in Santen (C-673/18). The new reference continues this long running saga. The fact scenario is as follows. Boehringer Ingelheim Vetmedica GmbH holds a patent which protects the use of ciclesonide for treating asthma in horses, along with a veterinary medicinal product authorisation, in accordance with Directive 2001/82/EC, for the marketing of the product Aservo® Equihaler®. This was the first authorised veterinary medicinal product containing ciclesonide as an active ingredient, as acknowledged by the European Medicines Agency (EMA) during the authorisation procedure. However, ciclesonide had already been approved in 2005 for use as a human medicine, for the treatment of asthma (albeit from another manufacturer). Boehringer applied for an SPC in Germany but this was rejected by the German Patent Office (DPMA) in view of the prior marketing authorisation for the human medicine. The DPMA said that following its decision in Santen (C-673/18), the CJEU “abandoned” its previous case law under Neurim (C-130/11), meaning that the assessment of whether an authorisation is a “first authorisation” within the meaning of Art 3(d) of the SPC Regulation must be examined in relation to the active ingredient and not in relation to the therapeutic use of the product. In appealing the DPMA’s decision, Boehringer argued that there should be a strict regulatory distinction between an authorisation as a veterinary medicinal product and one for a medicinal product for human use. It also argued (as was contended in Neurim, albeit the other way around) that because clinical studies in humans cannot be used for the initial authorisation of a veterinary product, one of the objectives of the SPC Regulation would be frustrated were no SPC to be available in this case. Santen and Abraxis (C-443/17) were distinguished as relating to scenarios where both earlier and later approvals were for medicinal products for human use. Pharmacia (C-31/03) was said to concern only questions relating to transitional provisions of the original Regulation (EC) 1768/92. In making the reference, the BPatG observed inconsistencies in the approaches taken across Europe (specifically, in the Czech Republic, France and the Netherlands), such that “the correct application of Community Law is not so obvious that there is no room for reasonable doubt”. It also stated firstly, that it “tends to regard” the veterinary authorisation as the first (so paving the way for a valid SPC grant); second, that it is plausible that the Neurim decision will be upheld (despite Santen, as the underlying facts were “very different”), and third, that a distinction between human and veterinary authorisations could validly be made. In an echo of arguments made by Neurim to the CJEU back in 2011, the BPatG observes that were no SPC to be granted in these circumstances, the pharmaceutical industry could be discouraged from researching the possibility of using certain active substances for humans if corresponding products for animals had already been authorised, and vice versa. In concluding, I would note an interesting observation in a fairly recent (Jan 2025) judgment from the Court of Appeal of England and Wales, in which Lord Justice Birss credited Santen as bringing “ a measure of coherence ” to the scheme of the SPC regulation whilst also substantially reducing the “ legal uncertainty caused by Neurim ”. However, it is clear that uncertainty continues across Europe, and one reason behind the BPatG sending this issue up to the CJEU stems from the apparent inconsistency in the pattern of SPC grants and refusals on this product, from the various Patent Offices where the requirements of Article 3(d) of the SPC Regulation are examined. Finally, another observation from the Court of Appeal, this one by Lord Justice Jacob all those years ago when he referred Neurim up to the CJEU (Neurim Pharmaceuticals (1991) Ltd v Comptroller-General of Patents (EWCA Civ 1064, para 68)): “In short, if Neurim are wrong, then the Regulation will not have achieved its key objects for large areas of pharmaceutical research: it will not be fit for purpose.” If “Neurim” is replaced by “Boehringer”, this statement might equally apply to the facts of this case now before the CJEU. The question referred runs as follows: “Is Article 3(d) of Regulation (EC) No 469/2009 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 6 May 2009 concerning the supplementary protection certificate for medicinal products to be interpreted as meaning that the marketing authorisation for a product as a veterinary medicinal product in accordance with Directive 2001/82/EC is the first marketing authorisation for that product as a medicinal product, even if a marketing authorisation for the same active substance as a medicinal product for human use has previously been granted in accordance with Directive 2001/83/EC?.”
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 德国联邦专利法院就补充保护证书（SPC）条例第 3(d) 条中“首份上市许可”的定义向欧盟法院提起新预审。案件涉及勃林格殷格翰的兽药产品，虽为该活性成分首个兽药许可，但该成分此前已获人用药品许可。德国专利局依据 Santen 案判例驳回申请，认为应基于活性成分而非治疗用途判断，此案旨在厘清人兽用药许可交叉时的 SPC 资格争议。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企在欧布局兽药或人药专利时，需警惕活性成分跨领域使用导致的 SPC 申请风险。建议密切关注本案裁决，若欧盟法院维持严格标准，应调整欧洲市场准入与专利延期组合策略，避免因 prior art 许可丧失独占期。

news_items.title: RELIEF - CJEU confirms EUIPO practice contrary to AG Cápeta opinion in APE TEES
news_items.title_zh: CJEU 确认 EUIPO 实践：在先权利须在裁决时依然有效
news_items.summary: We reported on Advocate General (AG) Cápeta’s opinion in the APE TEES case (C-337/22 P, aka Nowhere, the name of the applicant before the GC) here expressing the hope – in fact expectation – that the CJEU would not follow that opinion. We are very relieved to report that this expectation has not been disappointed.
news_items.content: RELIEF - CJEU confirms EUIPO practice contrary to AG Cápeta opinion in APE TEES We reported on Advocate General (AG) Cápeta’s opinion in the APE TEES case (C-337/22 P, aka Nowhere, the name of the applicant before the GC) here expressing the hope – in fact expectation – that the CJEU would not follow that opinion. We are very relieved to report that this expectation has not been disappointed. Commercial logic and equity prevailed over formalism. In today’s ruling, the CJEU confirmed in clear terms (para. 173): [The] earlier right must continue to exist until the date on which EUIPO rules on the opposition based on that right, including at the stage of the appeal before the Board of Appeal […]. In this case, the earlier right (an alleged unregistered UK trademark right) had ceased to be an “earlier right” in the EU following Brexit. However, the question before the CJEU was not limited to Brexit situations but was much broader, concerning the loss of validity of an earlier right in the course of the proceedings for whichever reason, for example non-use cancellation, surrender, or expiry. The EUIPO has always considered that the earlier right must be valid and enforceable at the time of the decision on the substance. Of course, the earlier right must also have been valid and enforceable at the application or priority date of the contested trademark (application), for opposition and invalidity proceedings alike, as otherwise there is no prior right to begin with. Both dates are relevant. Where the earlier right loses validity in between, there is no longer a conflict between valid rights and the owner of the (former) prior right has no legitimate interest in preventing or cancelling the registration of a later trademark. The CJEU’s confirmation of this understanding of the EUIPO is rotund. The various arguments made to justify the contrary understanding, namely, that an opposition or invalidity action should be granted even if the earlier mark, at the time of the decision, no longer exists, are one by one taken apart, resulting in a textbook judgment of 182 paragraphs. For those interested in EU trademark proceedings, however, the main message is simple: if the earlier mark “goes away”, so does the conflict, and there is no ground for refusal or invalidity of a later trademark. This means for the appeal in SHOPPI (earlier posts on that see here and here ) that it will inevitably fail if it is not withdrawn. In that case, the GC had taken exactly the position now confirmed by the CJEU. That SHOPPI concerns an invalidity action and not an opposition makes no difference – the CJEU repeatedly refers to both opposition and invalidity proceedings alike. The judgment is also binding for national IP offices in the EU Member States – which, to the knowledge of this author, means that no change in practice is required. Famously, the UKIPO follows the opposite approach, considering only the status of the earlier mark at the application or priority date of the mark under attack. UK practitioners often defend that approach (see for example here ) – but where a UK application from before the end of 2020 fails, after drawn out proceedings, only because of an earlier EU trademarks that was and is used only in the EU, that is (rightly) perceived as unfair.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院在 APE TEES 案中作出判决，驳回总检察长意见，明确支持 EUIPO 长期实践：商标异议或无效程序中，在先权利不仅需在申请日有效，还必须在 EUIPO 作出实质裁决时依然存续。若期间因脱欧、撤销或未续展等原因失效，则冲突消失，后续商标可获注册。该原则统一适用于异议与无效程序，并约束成员国局。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧维权需动态监控对手商标状态，若对方权利在审理期失效可直接胜诉。反之，若自身为在先权利人，务必维持权利有效性至终局裁决，避免因疏忽导致维权失败。

news_items.title: Recuperate part of the application fee through the SME voucher
news_items.title_zh: 中小企业可通过代金券报销部分比荷卢商标申请费
news_items.summary: The SME voucher enables entrepreneurs to recuperate part of the application fee for trademark and design registrations.
news_items.content: The SME voucher enables entrepreneurs to recuperate part of the application fee for trademark and design registrations. This is a European Commission initiative, implemented by the EUIPO (European Union Intellectual Property Office). The campaign runs in principle until 4 December 2026. The funding available for the subsidy scheme may be used up before this date. Through the SME voucher you can recuperate up to 75% of the costs of a trademark or design application with BOIP. Read more about the different reductions on our ‘SME-voucher’ promotional page Was this information helpful? No What do you think we can improve? Was this information helpful? Yes Thank you! Please let us know what you liked or what could be improved. Do you have other questions? Please contact our Information Centre .
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 比荷卢知识产权局（BOIP）宣布，欧盟委员会发起并由 EUIPO 实施的“中小企业代金券”计划再次开放。该计划允许企业家报销高达 75% 的商标或外观设计申请费用。活动原则上持续至 2026 年 12 月 4 日，但资金可能在截止日期前用完。此政策旨在降低中小企业在比荷卢地区的知识产权保护成本，鼓励品牌创新与设计注册。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国中小出口企业及在欧初创团队应充分利用此政策降低品牌布局成本。建议立即评估比荷卢市场商标需求，尽早提交申请以锁定补贴额度，避免因资金耗尽而错失良机，同时注意合规使用代金券流程。

news_items.title: The UPC's LD Hamburg remains firm on late-filed submissions (UPC_CFI_461/2024; UPC_CFI_718/2024)
news_items.title_zh: UPC 汉堡分院坚持驳回逾期提交的无效攻击与证据
news_items.summary: Court docket: LD
news_items.content: Hamburg , decision of 05.11.2025 UPC_CFI_ 461 /2024; UPC_CFI_ 718 /2024 [ EP 2476814 ] Parties: Dolle A/S v. FAKRO Dachflächenfenster GmbH Decision seems of interest regarding the front-loaded nature of the proceedings before the UPC and the admissibility of late-filed evidence and attacks. Dolle A/S v. F akro Dachflächenfenster GmbH examines a n infringement claim with a counterclaim for revocation. Infringement and revocation were examined by the same Local Division (no bifurcation ) . The patent in question, EP 2 476 814 B1 , was granted to Dolle . The decision is interesting regarding the admissibility of late-filed facts and grounds for revocation (h eadnote 2 ). The decision highlights that attacks against validity must be introduced into the proceedings at the same time as the counterclaim for revocation , with strict restrictions on subsequently filed attacks or evidence . When the counterclaim for revocation was introduced, the defendant ( Fakro ) raised a number of novelty attacks and inventive step attacks. Subsequent to filing the counterclaim, the defendant also put forward late-filed facts and evidence and a late-filed attack . These new facts , evidence and arguments were not admitted into proceedings. Late-filed facts /evidence N ew documents were filed a fter the filing of the counterclaim for revocation. To justify the late filing of the documents , Fakro argued that this was the result of an omission in a list of research hits by two search agencies . The Division left the question open as to whether the documents could be admitted as ultimately they did not constitute prior art . Even if t he Division left the question open, our impression when reading the reasons is that the Division seem ed un convinced by the arguments , given that the documents originated from the plaintiff (Dolle) and should have been subject to closer scrutiny . Late-filed grounds As for the late-file d ground, Fakro challenged , for the first time in the oral hearing , inventive step over a prior use already part of the proceedings , in the attacks for a lack of novelty . T he Division did not examine the new attack and rejected the objection as inadmissible , despite the attack being based on evidence already in the proceedings. The Division emphasised the “ front-loaded procedural system ” , to allow for the other party to be able to prepare an adequate defence and, at the same time, expedite the proceedings. The Division then concluded that “in principle ”, a counterclaimant cannot introduce new attack s or new documents at the oral hearing as this would make it impossible for the other party to prepare. This stance appears stricter than the usual approach of the EPO’s Opposition Divisions , which is unsurprising given the UPC’s focus on expedited proceedings. In summary, t he main takeaway s of this decision are: (1) that new evidence filed after the initial statement of defence and counterclaim will not be admitted unless there are compelling reasons for not filing the evidence earlier , (2) that particular attention should be paid to any evidence belonging to one of the parties, as they are less likely to be viewed as admissible at a later stage , and ( 3 ) that new grounds for invalidity , even if based on evidence already in proceedings, will not be admitted if the other parties have had little or no ti me to prepare .
news_items.ai_summary_zh: UPC 汉堡地方法院在 Dolle 诉 FAKRO 案中裁定，被告在提出撤销反诉后逾期提交的新事实、证据及无效理由不予采纳。法院强调 UPC 程序的前置加载特性，要求所有无效攻击必须随反诉一并提出，口头审理阶段原则上禁止引入新攻击，以确保对方防御权并加速诉讼进程。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对 UPC 诉讼时，必须在答辩初期穷尽所有无效证据与理由，切勿依赖后续补充。需建立前置化诉讼策略，避免因程序规则严苛导致实体权利丧失，提升海外维权精准度。
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 严格限制逾期证据，无效攻击须随撤销反诉同步提出", "口头审理阶段原则上禁止引入新的无效理由或文件", "该立场比 EPO 异议部更严，旨在保障程序效率与公平"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["务必在提起撤销反诉时一次性提交全部无效证据", "适应 UPC 前置程序，避免沿用 EPO 后期补正习惯"]

news_items.title: European SEP developments spur calls to add UK, Germany and UPC to US patent watch list
news_items.title_zh: 欧洲 SEP 动态引发将英德及 UPC 列入美国专利观察名单呼声
news_items.summary: As standard essential patent policy debates escalate, some stakeholders advocate for adding European jurisdictions on the USTR watch list
news_items.content: As standard essential patent policy debates escalate, some stakeholders advocate for adding European jurisdictions on the USTR watch list
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 随着标准必要专利（SEP）政策辩论升级，部分利益相关方呼吁美国贸易代表办公室（USTR）将英国、德国及统一专利法院（UPC）列入特别 301 报告观察名单。此举旨在施压欧洲司法辖区调整 FRAND 许可规则，反映跨大西洋在 SEP 执法与定价机制上的深层分歧，可能重塑全球专利许可格局。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临更复杂的地缘政治化专利环境。需警惕美欧规则冲突导致的合规风险，建议提前评估英德诉讼策略，建立多元化 FRAND 抗辩预案，避免成为大国博弈牺牲品。

news_items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局与欧洲司法合作组织在海牙联合举办第十届知识产权犯罪高层会议。此次会议旨在强化欧盟内部协作机制，汇聚多方代表共同应对日益严峻的知识产权犯罪挑战，通过提升执法效率保护欧盟的创新成果与市场竞争力，彰显了欧盟在知识产权刑事保护领域的坚定承诺。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中企需警惕欧盟升级的刑事执法力度，完善内部合规体系以防侵权风险。出口企业应建立知识产权预警机制，避免因无意侵权面临刑事调查，同时可借助欧盟协作网络维护自身合法权益。

news_items.title: Limiting the unitary patent, losing the long arm?
news_items.title_zh: 限制单位专利是否导致丧失长臂管辖权？
news_items.summary: IMC Créations v Mul-T-Lock France. UPC_CFI_702/2024; UPC_CFI_369/2025
news_items.content: Limiting the unitary patent, losing the long arm? IMC Créations v Mul-T-Lock France – Local Division Paris, 16 January 2026 (UPC_CFI_702/2024; UPC_CFI_369/2025) Introduction The Paris Local Division dealt with infringement of a European patent with unitary effect and additional national validations including Switzerland. The patent had been unconditionally limited during the UPC proceedings. The Court upheld the patent in its limited form and found infringement within the UPC territory. Of particular interest are the temporal effect of the limitation and the consequences for enforcement of the Swiss national part. Temporal effect of the unconditional limitation The Court held that the unconditional limitation of the Unitary Patent took effect retroactively from the date of grant of the European patent. The limited claim set therefore governed the assessment of infringement in the UPC territory for the entire period since grant, not only from the date on which the limitation was introduced into the proceedings. Arguments based on legal certainty for acts committed before the limitation were not accepted. Limitation and the Swiss national part The Court drew a strict distinction between the Unitary Patent and the Swiss part of the European patent. Infringement of the Swiss national part had to be assessed on the basis of the patent as granted, since the limitation concerned only the Unitary Patent and did not affect the Swiss validation. However, the Court did not go on to assess Swiss infringement. It reasoned that the patent, in its granted form, faced a non-negligible risk of revocation before the competent Swiss authorities in light of the proprietor’s decision to unconditionally limit the Unitary Patent. At the same time, the UPC has no jurisdiction to decide on the validity of the Swiss part. In this situation, the Court considered it inappropriate to rule on infringement of the Swiss part and rejected that part of the case. In detail, the Court indicated that, where a serious validity risk exists for a non-UPC designation, the proper course would in principle be to stay the infringement assessment pending a decision by the courts of the state of grant. In the present case, however, no revocation action was pending in Switzerland. The Court therefore considered a stay not possible and, instead of suspending the Swiss part of the case, dismissed it outright. This approach effectively turns the absence of parallel Swiss validity proceedings into a reason not to exercise long-arm jurisdiction, leaving the proprietor without a UPC ruling on Swiss infringement despite the UPC having otherwise accepted long-arm competence in principle. The decision shows that a limitation that strengthens the patent position within the UPC territory may at the same time weaken the prospects of long-arm enforcement for non-UPC states, because it accentuates the divergence between the limited UP claim set and the still unamended national claims. A potential solution to such a situation may be to seek limitation centrally before the EPO under Art. 105b EPC, so that the amended claim set also governs the non-UPC validations. Takeaways An unconditional limitation of a Unitary Patent applies from the date of grant and governs infringement in the UPC territory for the entire period thereafter. For non-UPC states, infringement is assessed on the patent as granted (or as amended in opposition or limitation proceedings before the EPO). If the limitation of the UPC parts (or, as here, the UP) cast doubt on the validity of the broader granted claims, the UPC may refuse to rule on infringement of non-UPC parts where it cannot assess validity itself. Consider filing a request for limitation at the EPO (Art. 105b EPC) before relying on the UPC’s long-arm jurisdiction for non-UPC states. IMC Créations v Mul-T-Lock France – Local Division Paris, 16 January 2026 (UPC_CFI_702/2024; UPC_CFI_369/2025) The Paris Local Division dealt with infringement of a European patent with unitary effect and additional national validations including Switzerland. The patent had been unconditionally limited during the UPC proceedings. The Court upheld the patent in its limited form and found infringement within the UPC territory. Of particular interest are the temporal effect of the limitation and the consequences for enforcement of the Swiss national part. The Court held that the unconditional limitation of the Unitary Patent took effect retroactively from the date of grant of the European patent. The Court drew a strict distinction between the Unitary Patent and the Swiss part of the European patent. Infringement of the Swiss national part had to be assessed on the basis of the patent as granted, since the limitation concerned only the Unitary Patent and did not affect the Swiss validation. However, the Court did not go on to assess Swiss infringement. At the same time, the UPC has no jurisdiction to decide on the validity of the Swiss part. In detail, the Court indicated that, where a serious validity risk exists for a non-UPC designation, the proper course would in principle be to stay the infringement assessment pending a decision by the courts of the state of grant. In the present case, however, no revocation action was pending in Switzerland. The Court therefore considered a stay not possible
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 巴黎地方法院审理 IMC 诉 Mul-T-Lock 案，裁定单位专利的无条件限制具有追溯力，自授权日起生效。法院区分了单位专利与瑞士国家部分，因权利人限制单位专利导致瑞士部分存在无效风险，且无并行瑞士诉讼，法院拒绝行使长臂管辖权驳回瑞士部分诉求。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧维权需谨慎处理单位专利限制策略。单方面限制虽可稳固欧盟境内权利，但可能导致无法通过 UPC 一并追究瑞士等非成员国侵权责任，增加跨国维权成本与复杂性。

news_items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局宣布对 PACE（欧洲专利申请加速审查计划）进行重大修订，新版本将于 2026 年 2 月 1 日正式生效。该更新已通过 2025 年 12 月官方期刊公告发布，旨在对欧洲专利申请的加速程序进行结构性调整，以优化审查流程效率。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需提前评估现有加速请求策略，适应新程序结构以避免审查延误。建议密切关注 EPO 后续操作指南，调整高价值专利布局节奏，确保在过渡期内合规利用加速通道。

news_items.title: EUIPO launches 2026 SME Fund - GJE
news_items.title_zh: EUIPO 启动 2026 年中小企业知识产权基金
news_items.summary: EU SMEs can access €7,000+ in IP funding through the EUIPO 2026 SME Fund for patents, trade marks, designs and related services.
news_items.content:
About us About GJE Diversity & Inclusion Work at GJE Vacancies

Our expertise Patents Trade Marks Designs Life Sciences & Biotech Chemistry & Pharma Computer Technology & AI Physics & Engineering Industrial Design & Consumer Products Branding & Trade Marks

Industrial Design & Consumer Products

Our approach Securing the best future for your business Supporting your in-house IP function Providing European IP expertise for you and your client Building confidence in your investment decisions

Securing the best future for your business

Providing European IP expertise for you and your client

On 2 February 2026, the European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) launched the 2026 edition of its SME Fund. The SME Fund is a European Commission initiative that provides EU-based small and medium-sized enterprises with funding for eligible IP services.

Any EU-based company that meets the EU definition of a SME , that is, it has fewer than 250 employees and an annual turnover of less than €50 million or a balance sheet of less than €43 million, will be eligible for the fund.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局于 2026 年 2 月 2 日启动新版中小企业基金，由欧盟委员会发起。该计划为符合定义的欧盟中小企业提供超 7000 欧元资助，覆盖专利、商标、外观设计及相关服务费用。申请主体需满足员工少于 250 人且营收或资产负债表低于特定阈值的条件，旨在降低企业知识产权保护成本。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧运营的中国中小企业应积极评估资格，利用该基金降低布局成本。建议提前准备合规证明材料，结合商标与专利策略同步申请，最大化政策红利以增强市场竞争力。

news_items.title: KMU-Fonds 2026: EU-Förderprogramm für kleine und mittlere Unternehmen startet wieder
news_items.title_zh: 欧盟 2026 年中小企业基金重启：资助知识产权扫描与申请
news_items.summary: KMU-Fonds 2026: EU-Förderprogramm für kleine und mittlere Unternehmen startet wieder - Individueller IP-Scan, Anmeldung von Patenten, Marken, Designs und Sorten sowie Stand-der-Technik-Recherchen: Kleine und mittelgroße Unternehmen erhalten EU-Förderung, um ihr geistiges Eigentum zu schützen – Laut KMU-Barometer verfügen nur 10 Prozent der kleinen Unternehmen über Schutzrechte – DPMA-Präsidentin: Nur wer geistiges Eigentum schützt, kann dessen volles wirtschaftliches Potenzial ausschöpfen - Das
news_items.content: Inhalt KMU-Fonds 2026: EU-Förderprogramm für kleine und mittlere Unternehmen startet wieder Individueller IP-Scan, Anmeldung von Patenten, Marken, Designs und Sorten sowie Stand-der-Technik-Recherchen: Kleine und mittelgroße Unternehmen erhalten EU-Förderung, um ihr geistiges Eigentum zu schützen – Laut KMU-Barometer verfügen nur 10 Prozent der kleinen Unternehmen über Schutzrechte – DPMA-Präsidentin: Nur wer geistiges Eigentum schützt, kann dessen volles wirtschaftliches Potenzial ausschöpfen Pressemitteilung vom 2. Februar 2026 München. Um ihr geistiges Eigentum zu ermitteln und zu schützen, können kleine und mittelgroße Unternehmen ab sofort (Start: 2. Februar) wieder europäisches Fördergeld beantragen. Mit dem auch 2026 neu aufgelegten KMU-Fonds gewährt das Amt der Europäischen Union für geistiges Eigentum (EUIPO) Zuschüsse unter anderem für sogenannte IP-Scans, also grundlegende Untersuchungen zum jeweiligen Potenzial, geistiges Eigentum zu schützen. Zudem übernimmt das EUIPO einen Großteil der Kosten für Patent-, Marken- und Designanmeldungen und für Stand-der-Technik-Recherchen. Zuschüsse gibt es auch für Sortenschutzanmeldungen. „Nur wer sein geistiges Eigentum wirksam schützt, kann dessen volles wirtschaftliches Potenzial ausschöpfen“ , betont die Präsidentin des Deutschen Patent- und Markenamts, Eva Schewior. „Kleine und mittlere Unternehmen bilden das Fundament unserer Volkswirtschaft. Viele dieser Unternehmen könnten sich noch besser entwickeln, wenn sie die Chancen durch den Schutz geistigen Eigentums erkennen und konsequent nutzen.“ Das DPMA koordiniert das Förderprogramm auf deutscher Ebene. Schutzrechte des geistigen Eigentums wie Patente, Gebrauchsmuster, Marken und Designs schützen gegen Nachahmung, sichern Investitionen ab und machen Unternehmen attraktiver für Investoren. Laut dem KMU-Barometer des EUIPO bestehen rund zwei Drittel der Arbeitsplätze in der Europäischen Union in kleinen und mittleren Unternehmen. Diese erzielen zudem mehr als die Hälfte der EU-Wertschöpfung. 93 Prozent der europäischen KMU, die Schutzrechte des geistigen Eigentums nutzen, sehen laut der Studie dadurch eine ausschließlich positive Wirkung. Das Problem: Gerade einmal rund zehn Prozent der KMU verfügen derzeit über solche Schutzrechte. Unternehmen, die eine Förderung aus dem KMU-Fonds 2026 erhalten möchten, sollten sich zunächst auf den Internetseiten des KMU-Fonds anmelden und den Antrag stellen. Darauf sollten sie den Finanzhilfebescheid (Gutschein) des EUIPO abwarten und den Gutschein aktivieren. Im nächsten Schritt können sie Patent, Marke, Design oder Sorte anmelden, einen IP-Scan-Termin vereinbaren oder eine Stand-der-Technik-Recherche in Auftrag geben. Die Dienstleistungen sollten innerhalb der vom EUIPO vorgegebenen Frist durchgeführt werden. Danach können die Unternehmen einen Erstattungsantrag mit Originalrechnung/Kostenbeleg im KMU-Fonds-Nutzerbereich beim EUIPO einreichen. Im KMU-Fonds 2026 stehen insgesamt 23 Millionen Euro zum Abruf bereit. In den vergangenen fünf Jahren wurden in der gesamten EU rund 98.000 Unternehmen durch den Fonds gefördert, rund 10.000 davon aus Deutschland. KMU sind nach der zugrundeliegenden Definition selbständige Unternehmen mit maximal 250 Mitarbeitern und einem Umsatz von bis zu 50 Millionen Euro oder einer Bilanzsumme von bis zu 43 Millionen Euro (vergleiche EU-Kommission-Empfehlung Dokument 2003/361/EC). Weitere Informationen zum KMU-Fonds finden Sie auf unseren Internetseiten . Das Deutsche Patent- und Markenamt Erfindergeist und Kreativität brauchen wirksamen Schutz. Das DPMA ist das deutsche Kompetenzzentrum für alle Schutzrechte des geistigen Eigentums - für Patente, Gebrauchsmuster, Marken und Designs. Als größtes nationales Patentamt in Europa und sechstgrößtes nationales Patentamt der Welt steht es für die Zukunft des Erfinderlandes Deutschland in einer globalisierten Wirtschaft. Seine rund 2.800 Mitarbeiterinnen und Mitarbeiter an drei Standorten - München, Jena und Berlin - sind Dienstleister für Erfinder und Unternehmen. Sie setzen Innovationsstrategien des Bundes um und entwickeln die nationalen, europäischen und internationalen Schutzsysteme weiter. Bild: EUIPO Stand: 02.02.2026
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）于 2026 年 2 月重启中小企业基金，旨在提升欧洲中小企业知识产权持有率。该计划提供资金用于 IP 扫描、现有技术研究及专利、商标、外观设计和植物新品种的申请费用补贴。德国专利商标局（DPMA）负责德国区协调。数据显示仅 10% 中小企业拥有知识产权，而使用者中 93% 获益显著。企业需先在线申请代金券，激活后开展服务并提交报销。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国中小企业应充分利用该基金降低合规与布局成本。建议立即评估自身技术品牌资产，通过 IP 扫描明确保护策略，利用补贴完成高价值专利或商标的欧洲注册，提升投资吸引力并防范侵权风险。

news_items.title: Trademark News: What you might have missed in January 2025
news_items.title_zh: 2025 年 1 月商标要闻：尼斯分类更新与丹麦费用调整
news_items.summary: Stay informed with our comprehensive roundup of the past month's headlines, all neatly compiled for you in one convenient post. Catch up on the latest news reported by the Kluwer IP Reporter.
news_items.content: Trademark News: What you might have missed in January 2025 Stay informed with our comprehensive roundup of the past month's headlines, all neatly compiled for you in one convenient post. Catch up on the latest news reported by the Kluwer IP Reporter. The 2026 version of the 13th edition of the International Classification of Goods and Services for the Purposes of the Registration of Marks (Nice Classification), which was released recently by the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) and came into force on 1 January 2026, has introduced several significant changes regarding common product categories. The Argentine National Institute of Industrial Property has issued Resolution 583/2025, introducing changes to trademark examination and registration procedures with immediate effect and announcing changes for examination scope and further procedural changes with effect from 1 March 2026. In December 2025, Chinese lawmakers submitted a draft amendment to the Trademark Law for first review before the Standing Committee of the National People’s Congress to address emerging challenges in trademark protection. On 15 December 2025, the Government of Grenada acceded to the Protocol Relating to the Madrid Agreement Concerning the International Registration of Marks by depositing its instrument of accession with the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO), becoming the 116th member of the Madrid System and increasing the system’s reach to 132 countries. On 22 December 2025, the Intellectual Property Department of Hong Kong (Hong Kong IP Office) launched a new image search service called the Image Mark Finder (iMF). The Intellectual Property Department of Hong Kong has recently announced that it will launch a new image-based trademark search service called Image Mark Finder (iMF) by the end of 2025. Pursuant to the provisions of the Common Regulations under the Madrid Agreement and Protocol Concerning the International Registration of Marks, the Director General of the World Intellectual Property Organization on 17 December 2025 announced the revised amounts of individual fees applicable when Denmark is designated in an international application or in an application for renewal of international registration or is a subject of subsequent designation. The revised fees will enter into force with respect to Denmark on 1 January 2026 and 1 July 2026. The New Zealand Intellectual Property Office has recently updated its guidance on replacing national trademarks with international registrations, following the March 2025 amendments to the Trade Marks (International Registration) Regulations 2012.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 本文汇总 2025 年 1 月全球商标动态，核心涉欧内容包括：WIPO 发布 2026 版尼斯分类并于年初生效；丹麦作为马德里体系成员调整单独规费，分两阶段实施；此外涵盖阿根廷、中国、格林纳达及新西兰等地程序修订。虽为全球综述，但丹麦费用及分类标准直接关联欧洲商标布局成本与规范。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需关注新版尼斯分类对商品表述的影响，及时修正申请策略。针对丹麦费用调整，应评估在欧马德里延伸成本变化，优化预算。利用格林纳达新成员身份，可更低成本拓展加勒比市场保护。

news_items.title: Munich Regional Court further clarifies FRAND rules in Wilus vs Asus judgment
news_items.title_zh: 慕尼黑法院在 Wilus 诉华硕案中进一步澄清 FRAND 规则
news_items.summary: A recent judgment in Asus vs Wilus by Munich Regional Court is peppered with guiding principles for the Chamber's FRAND jurisprudence.
news_items.content:
Firms & Experts Sponsored: Firm Profiles WEICKMANN Simmons + Simmons LLP ARNOLD RUESS View all Firm Profiles Sponsored: Expert Profiles Prof. Dr. Heinz Goddar Chris Thornham Prof. Dr. Pablo Fernández Carballo-Calero View all Expert Profiles Sponsored Content UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP View all Sponsored Content

Prof. Dr. Pablo Fernández Carballo-Calero

UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP

The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

A partial payment obligation, comparative agreements for FRAND licences and FRAND calculation. A recent judgment by Munich Regional Court's 7th Civil Chamber is peppered with guiding principles for the chamber's FRAND jurisprudence. The judges ordered Asus to cease and desist due to the infringement of a Wilus wifi patent. In future, the chamber will not only look at the implementer's willingness to take a licence but also at the FRAND conformity of the SEP holder's offer.

Implementer Asus, SEP holder Wilus and their lawyers received a judgment yesterday that packs a punch. Munich Regional Court ordered Asus to cease and desist from infringing the Wifi 6 patent EP 3 512 289 . This much had been known since mid-January. Ever since, Asus has not been allowed to sell certain Wifi-6-compatible devices in Germany (case ID: Ref. 7 O 5007/25).

These include the Zenfone 11 Ultra smartphone, the ASUS Zenbook S 13, the ASUS Chromebox 4 mini PC and the ASUS AiO E3 (E3402) all-in-one PC. However, the plaintiff, the South Korean Wilus Institute of Standards and Technology, has not yet enforced the judgment. Asus can also appeal, which is considered certain.

The background to the lawsuit is the conclusion of a licence agreement for Sisvel’s Wifi 6 portfolio or bilaterally with the owner of the patent Wilus and SK Telecom. Last year, the South Korean R&D lab also launched a separate action against Asus at the Mannheim UPC local division alleging infringement of a second Wifi 6 patent.

Yesterday, the opposing parties and their lawyers received the written judgment with many fundamental comments, some of which went beyond the case. The 7th Civil Chamber has not had the opportunity to solidify its FRAND guideline for some time as many recent SEP cases have settled early. What the judges have now written into the Wilus vs Asus judgment is groundbreaking for future SEP proceedings at Munich Regional Court.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 慕尼黑地区法院第七民事庭就 Wilus 诉华硕案作出判决，裁定华硕侵犯 WiFi 6 专利并须停止销售相关设备。该案核心意义在于法院确立了新的 FRAND  jurisprudence 指导原则：未来不仅审查实施者的许可意愿，还将严格评估 SEP 持有人的报价是否符合 FRAND 原则。尽管原告尚未执行禁令且被告预计上诉，但该判决为德国后续 SEP 诉讼奠定了重要基准。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临更平衡的 SEP 诉讼环境，需重视双向合规。建议出口企业不仅要证明自身许可意愿，更要建立对权利人报价进行 FRAND 合规性抗辩的能力，避免因单方让步陷入被动，同时关注上诉进程以调整市场策略。
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院裁定华硕侵权并禁止其在德国销售特定 WiFi 6 兼容设备", "确立新规则：将同时审查实施者意愿与权利人报价的 FRAND 合规性", "此案填补了慕尼黑法院近期因和解频发而缺乏的 FRAND 判例空白"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["从单方审查转向双向评估，中国企业需提升报价合规抗辩能力", "面对禁令风险，应结合上诉策略与市场库存管理制定综合应对方案"]
news_items.ai_relevance_reason_zh: 慕尼黑法院判决明确 SEP/FRAND 规则，直接影响在德运营企业。

news_items.title: Nokia vs Asus and Acer: Regional Court Munich redefines FRAND rules
news_items.title_zh: 诺基亚诉华硕宏碁：慕尼黑地方法院重塑 FRAND 规则
news_items.summary: Munich Regional Court ordered Asus and Acer to cease and desist in an infringement case concerning a Nokia video coding patent.
news_items.content:
Firms & Experts Sponsored: Firm Profiles Zimmermann & Partner Patentanwälte mbB KATHER AUGENSTEIN VOSSIUS & PARTNER Patentanwälte Rechtsanwälte mbB View all Firm Profiles Sponsored: Expert Profiles Wilbert Derks Alexander Schlotter Dr. Léon Dijkman View all Expert Profiles Sponsored Content UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP View all Sponsored Content

Zimmermann & Partner Patentanwälte mbB

VOSSIUS & PARTNER Patentanwälte Rechtsanwälte mbB

UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP

The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Last Thursday, Munich Regional Court ordered Asus and Acer to cease and desist in an infringement case concerning a Nokia video coding patent. The court delivered its judgment on the same day as the hearing. The court indicated further modifications to the Munich FRAND rules.

Just yesterday, the Federal Court of Justice upheld an injunction by the Regional and Higher Regional Court of Munich against HMD, largely confirming current Munich FRAND case law. Only days earlier, the Munich Regional Court heard another FRAND case. The dispute between Nokia and computer manufacturers Asus and Acer concerned the infringement of Nokia’s EP 2 375 749 for video coding.

The 7th Civil Chamber at Munich Regional Court, under presiding judge Oliver Schön, heard the two lawsuits for a full day on 22 January. On the same day the judges found that Acer and Asus are not willing to take a licence and had infringed EP 749 (case IDs: Acer 7 O 4100/25 and Asus 7 O 4102/25). It is considered certain that Nokia will enforce the judgments and that both implementers will appeal.

In spring 2025, Nokia filed patent claims against Acer, Asus and Hisense at Munich Regional Court and the UPC over video codec patents. Nokia alleged that Acer and Asus’ PCs and Hisense’s smart TVs infringe three of its patents. While proceedings concerning EP 2 375 749 and EP 2 774 375 are pending at Munich Regional Court, Nokia filed an action regarding EP 2 661 892 at the UPC local division Munich. All relate to the H.265 video codec standard.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 慕尼黑地方法院就诺基亚视频编码专利侵权案，裁定华硕与宏碁停止侵权。法院认定被告不愿获得许可，同日宣判并进一步修正慕尼黑 FRAND 规则。此前联邦最高法院已确认类似禁令，诺基亚同时在统一专利法院提起相关诉讼，显示德国在 SEP 执法中的核心地位及规则趋严态势。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧使用视频编码标准需警惕高额禁令风险，应提前评估专利许可策略。面对德国法院趋严的 FRAND 审查，务必展现真诚谈判意愿，避免因被认定“非自愿被许可人”而遭禁售，同时关注 UPC 并行诉讼动态。

news_items.title: Strategic value of accurate data in increasingly complex 5G patent race
news_items.title_zh: 日益复杂的 5G 专利竞赛中准确数据的战略价值
news_items.summary: With 5G SEP licensing and litigation intensifying globally with courts across key jurisdictions increasingly asserting authority over FRAND rate setting and evaluating the conduct of licensors and implementers, patent portfolio assessments and comparative data are becoming critical reference points for negotiations and judicial analysis alike.
news_items.content:
The role of 5G has expanded well beyond consumer devices, underpinning a growing range of industrial IOT, automotive and infrastructure applications. This shift aligns with the evolution of standard toward 5G Advanced, starting with Release 18, which builds on core 5G technologies while introducing new capabilities, improved performance and broader industrial use cases. Patent activity reflects this transition in scale and scope: by 31 October 2025, more than 67,000 active 5G patent families had been granted worldwide, up from around 53,000 in 2024.

Figure 1. Cumulative number of worldwide granted 5G-declared patent families by year of first publication

At the same time, 5G SEP licensing and litigation have intensified globally, with courts across key jurisdictions increasingly asserting authority over FRAND rate setting and evaluating the conduct of licensors and implementers. As SEPs play a more central role in these disputes, patent portfolio assessments and comparative data are becoming critical reference points for negotiations and judicial analysis alike.

Continuity and change in the 2026 5G patent rankings

The new “ Who Is Leading the 5G Patent Race 2026 ” report provides an updated view of leadership in the global 5G patent landscape at a time when SEPs underpin an estimated US$15-billion annual licensing market. The race for 5G dominance is clearly reflected in public patent declarations and standards contribution submissions, which together form the analytical foundation of the analysis.

At the top of the 2026 rankings, leadership remains concentrated among a familiar group of companies when measured by the number of active and granted 5G-declared patent families. Huawei continues to hold the largest portfolio by volume, followed by Qualcomm.

However, the 2026 rankings also reveal notable movement beneath the surface when compared with last year’s analysis. Several companies strengthen or weaken their relative positions depending on whether portfolios are assessed by volume, value-adjusted metrics, technical contributions to 3GPP or across releases, technical working groups and geographic coverage. The full set of results is detailed in the “Top 50 5G Patent Rankings 2026 ”, which provides a broader view of how competitive positioning extends beyond the very top of the table.

Portfolio value reshapes perceptions of 5G leadership

Looking beyond simple patent-family counts, a value-adjusted view based on the Patent Asset Index provides a more differentiated perspective on 5G leadership. When 5G-declared patent families are assessed using this patent value metric, Huawei and Qualcomm – while both remaining at the top of the landscape – change their relative ordering compared with size-based rankings, and similar movements are visible for LG Electronics and Samsung. These differences illustrate why portfolio value has become an increasingly important reference point alongside volume when assessing competitive positioning in the 5G patent landscape.

Technical contributions add another dimension to 5G leadership
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 随着 5G 应用扩展至工业物联网与汽车领域，全球 SEP 许可与诉讼加剧，各国法院积极介入 FRAND 费率裁定。2026 年排名显示华为专利数量领先，但引入价值调整后格局变化。准确的数据评估已成为谈判与司法分析的关键参考，单纯数量不再是衡量领导力的唯一标准。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临更严苛的 FRAND 审查，需从追求专利数量转向质量与价值评估。建议利用精准数据进行组合分析，以应对诉讼风险并优化许可谈判策略，避免仅凭声明量盲目自信。

news_items.title: Federal Court of Justice hears FRAND appeal in VoiceAge vs HMD
news_items.title_zh: 德联邦最高法院审理 VoiceAge 诉 HMD 案中的 FRAND 上诉
news_items.summary: Today, the German Federal Court of Justice hears FRAND aspects in the appeal in VoiceAge vs HMD and will scrutinise Munich's FRAND approach.
news_items.content:
Firms & Experts Sponsored: Firm Profiles PAUSTIAN & PARTNER ARNOLD RUESS GOTTSCHALD View all Firm Profiles Sponsored: Expert Profiles Felix Hermann Harrison Chang, Ph.D. Sebastian Schneller View all Expert Profiles Sponsored Content UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP View all Sponsored Content

UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP

The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Today, the German Federal Court of Justice scrutinises Munich Higher Regional Court's new FRAND approach. The highest court's decision in the VoiceAge vs HMD case will have far-reaching consequences for European FRAND law. Once again, the EU Commission has intervened in the proceedings. JUVE Patent reporter Mathieu Klos is at the hearing.

After five years, FRAND is once again on the agenda in Karlsruhe. The city is home to the Federal Court of Justice, Germany’s highest civil court and final instance in patent proceedings. In 2020 and 2021, the court set out its interpretation of the CJEU’s European FRAND case law in Huawei vs ZTE with its two judgments Sisvel vs Haier I and II.

Today, the court will hear its next FRAND case in the appeal in VoiceAge vs HMD over EVS speech-coding patent EP 2 102 619 (case ID: KZR 10/25).

Previously, the Higher Regional Court Munich under presiding judge Lars Meinhardt found that HMD infringed VoiceAge’s EP 619 and is not a willing licensee (case ID: 6 U 3824/22 Kart).

The Regional Court Munich had also found HMD to be an unwilling licensee and ordered HMD to cease and desist (case ID: 7 O 14091/19 ).

HMD not only appealed to the Higher Regional Court Munich but also to the Federal Court of Justice in 2025. Given the fundamental importance of the FRAND-related issues, the Higher Regional Court Munich granted HMD leave to appeal to the Federal Court of Justice, but only concerning FRAND.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 德国联邦最高法院今日开庭审理 VoiceAge 诉 HMD 案的 FRAND 上诉，重点审查慕尼黑高等法院的新裁判思路。该案涉及语音编码专利，此前下级法院认定 HMD 为非自愿被许可人。欧盟委员会再次介入，最高院判决将对欧洲 FRAND 法律产生深远影响，重申对华为诉中兴案的解释。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临更严 FRAND 审查，需完善谈判记录以证“自愿”。建议密切关注卡尔斯鲁厄判决，调整许可策略，避免因被认定非自愿而遭禁令风险。

news_items.title: Geely seeking global FRAND rate for Nokia cellular SEPs in China
news_items.title_zh: 吉利在华起诉诺基亚寻求全球蜂窝 SEP 费率
news_items.summary: The move presents an opening for the Chinese courts to issue their second-ever global rate determination, more than two years after Oppo v Nokia
news_items.content: The move presents an opening for the Chinese courts to issue their second-ever global rate determination, more than two years after Oppo v Nokia
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 吉利已在中国法院提起诉讼，请求裁定其与诺基亚之间蜂窝标准必要专利的全球 FRAND 许可费率。此举继 OPPO 诉诺基亚案后，为中国法院第二次行使全球费率管辖权提供契机。该动态虽非欧洲直接司法判决，但因涉及跨国车企与欧洲专利巨头，将显著重塑中欧跨境知识产权博弈及全球许可规则走向。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国车企应善用国内司法管辖权反制海外禁令，平衡全球许可风险。欧洲企业需重新评估在华诉讼策略，避免单一法域裁决主导全球费率，建议加强中欧两地并行诉讼的协同应对与证据布局。

news_items.title: Sisvel launches Wi-Fi 7-era multimode patent pool
news_items.title_zh: Sisvel 推出 Wi-Fi 7 时代多模式专利池
news_items.summary: The new pool replaces the Wi-Fi 6 programme although existing agreements will continue, Sisvel’s David Muus tells IAM
news_items.content: The new pool replaces the Wi-Fi 6 programme although existing agreements will continue, Sisvel’s David Muus tells IAM
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲知名专利池运营商 Sisvel 宣布启动面向 Wi-Fi 7 时代的多模式专利池计划。该新池将取代原有的 Wi-Fi 6 项目，但现有签署的许可协议将继续有效。此举标志着无线通信标准必要专利许可进入新一代技术周期，旨在整合多模态技术标准，为被许可人提供覆盖最新 Wi-Fi 规范的统一许可方案，反映了欧洲 SEP 许可市场的动态演进。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国 Wi-Fi 终端及芯片企业需评估新池对许可成本的影响。建议提前梳理自有专利组合，积极参与费率谈判，利用 FRAND 原则规避不合理收费，并关注德国等关键法域的司法动向以制定应对策略。

news_items.ai_summary_zh: 诺基亚宣布与中国第五家汽车制造商达成 5G 和 Wi-Fi 标准必要专利许可协议，进一步扩大其在车联网领域的授权版图。与此同时，诺基亚针对吉利汽车的专利侵权诉讼仍在持续进行中。该动态反映了全球 SEP 权利人在汽车行业强化专利变现的策略，以及许可谈判与司法诉讼并行的常态。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国车企出海面临严峻 SEP 许可压力，需平衡谈判与诉讼风险。建议企业提前开展 FTO 分析，建立全球专利应对机制，避免在欧洲市场因禁令遭受重创。

news_items.title: Ask versus outcome: FRAND valuation, judicial analysis and the continuing gaps in SEP litigation
news_items.title_zh: 诉求与结果：FRAND 估值、司法分析及 SEP 诉讼中的持续差距
news_items.summary: IAM is a unique and timely intelligence service that treats IP as a business asset and tool rather than simply as a legal right, informing boardrooms worldwide.
news_items.content:
As disputes over SEPs and what constitutes FRAND terms continue to unfold in courts around the world, there is now a limited yet still significant body of case law ripe for analysis. A close examination of the available data provides valuable insight into how licensors' and licensees' initial royalty positions compare to the FRAND outcomes ultimately determined by courts.

Ongoing gap between licensors' asks, licensees' offers and court-set FRAND rates.

Courts providing clearer valuation through evidence-based analysis.

Limited but growing global case law providing clearer benchmarks for negotiations.

Judicial scrutiny reinforcing the importance of reliable comparables and disciplined analysis.

Continuing implications for 5G and future-generation licensing as valuation disputes persist.

Microsoft v Motorola, Case C10-1823JLR (WD Wash), 25 April 2013

TCL v Ericsson, Case 8:14-cv-00341-JVS (CD Cal), 21 December 2017

Unwired Planet v Huawei, [2017] EWHC 711 (Pat), 5 April 2017

Apple v Qualcomm, Case 3:17-cv-00108 (SD Cal), 16 April 2019
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析全球 SEP 诉讼中许可方要价、被许可方报价与法院最终裁定 FRAND 费率间的显著差距。通过梳理微软诉摩托罗拉等关键判例，指出司法正通过证据分析提供更清晰估值基准。尽管案例有限，但全球判例法正为谈判确立新标准，强调可靠可比数据的重要性，这对 5G 及未来技术许可具有深远影响。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧进行 SEP 许可谈判时，应摒弃极端报价策略，转而依据司法判例构建基于可靠可比数据的 disciplined 分析模型。建议提前研判欧美法院估值逻辑，以缩小诉求与裁决差距，降低诉讼风险并提升谈判成功率。

news_items.title_zh: 视频专利推动智能手机外 SEP 交易激增

news_items.content: Nokia has announced a string of deals, Access Advance and Via LA took major steps forward, while InterDigital redoubled its efforts
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 本文报道视频编码标准必要专利（SEP）正驱动许可交易超越智能手机领域。诺基亚宣布多项新协议，Access Advance 与 Via LA 专利池取得重大进展，InterDigital 亦加大布局。这标志着 SEP 许可重心向汽车、物联网等新兴领域扩展，欧洲权利人在其中扮演关键角色，全球许可格局正在重塑。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国车企及 IoT 厂商需警惕视频 SEP 许可风险，避免成为新目标。建议提前开展 FAND 合规评估，主动参与专利池谈判或寻求双边许可，防范在欧洲市场遭遇禁令或高额索赔。

news_items.title: JUB – Compétence confirmée dans l’affaire Sun Patent Trust c/ Vivo
news_items.title_zh: 统一专利法院巴黎分院确认 Sun 诉 Vivo 案管辖权
news_items.summary: JUB : la division locale de Paris a rendu une ordonnance rejetant l’exception d’incompétence soulevée par Vivo dans le litige l’opposant à Sun Patent Trust.
news_items.content:
Le 30 octobre 2025, la division locale de Paris de la Juridiction unifiée du brevet (JUB) a rendu une ordonnance (UPC_CFI_361/2025) rejetant l’exception d’incompétence soulevée par Vivo dans le litige l’opposant à Sun Patent Trust.

L’affaire concerne le brevet européen EP 3 852 468, relatif aux technologies 4G+, dont Sun Patent Trust reproche la contrefaçon par la commercialisation de divers modèles de smartphones Vivo en Europe.

Vivo soutenait que la JUB n’était pas compétente pour statuer sur la détermination des conditions FRAND, présentées par le demandeur comme partie intégrante de son action, et contestait également la compétence territoriale de la division parisienne.

Sun Patent Trust arguait au contraire qu’il s’agissait d’une action en contrefaçon de brevet essentiel (SEP), relevant de l’article 32(1)(a) UPCA, et que la question des conditions FRAND n’était pas une demande autonome mais une condition préalable à l’octroi d’une injonction.

Les magistrats de la division locale ont considéré que l’action principale était bien une action en contrefaçon et que les questions FRAND pouvaient être examinées à titre incident, conformément à la jurisprudence de la JUB et à l’arrêt Huawei v. ZTE. L’admissibilité de la sous-demande relative aux conditions FRAND sera tranchée au fond.

Sur le plan territorial, la Cour a jugé que l’achat d’un produit Vivo sur le site Fnac.com, destiné au marché français, suffisait à établir la compétence de la division locale de Paris au sens de l’article 33(1)(a) UPCA.

Cette ordonnance confirme la compétence de la JUB pour traiter les litiges SEP/FRAND dans le cadre d’actions en contrefaçon et illustre une approche pragmatique de la compétence territoriale, fondée sur des actes de commercialisation en ligne. L’affaire se poursuivra désormais sur le fond, avec un débat attendu sur la conformité des offres de licence aux exigences FRAND et sur l’octroi éventuel d’une injonction contre Vivo.

Céline Fabre 2026-03-17T08:47:11+01:00 17 mars 2026 | IP Alert Brevet , IP Alert Marque , Marques , Publication |

Céline Fabre 2026-02-17T09:31:26+01:00 16 février 2026 | Brevets , JUB , Publication |

Céline Fabre 2026-02-16T13:39:57+01:00 11 février 2026 | Brevets , IP Alert Brevet , Publication |
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 2025 年 10 月，统一专利法院（UPC）巴黎本地分院驳回 Vivo 提出的管辖权异议。法院认定，涉及标准必要专利（SEP）的侵权诉讼中，FRAND 许可条件可作为附带问题审理，且通过 Fnac 网站向法国销售产品足以确立地域管辖。此案确认了 UPC 处理 SEP/FRAND 纠纷的权限及基于在线销售的管辖原则。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国手机厂商需警惕 UPC 基于在线销售的广泛管辖权。在欧销售即便少量产品也可能引发全欧禁令风险。建议企业提前评估 SEP 许可策略，做好应对 UPC 诉讼及 FRAND 抗辩的准备，避免被动。

news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["在线销售行为极易触发 UPC 管辖，扩大被诉风险范围", "SEP 权利人可利用 UPC 程序施压，迫使接受全球许可条款"]

news_items.title: Wilus wins German injunction against Asustek over Wi-Fi 6 SEP
news_items.title_zh: Wilus 获德国法院针对华硕 Wi-Fi 6 SEP 禁令
news_items.summary: Korean patent owner strikes a blow in its latest enforcement campaign
news_items.content: Korean patent owner strikes a blow in its latest enforcement campaign
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 韩国专利持有者 Wilus 在德国针对华硕（Asustek）发起的标准必要专利（SEP）侵权诉讼中获胜，成功获得禁令救济。该案涉及 Wi-Fi 6 技术标准，标志着权利人在其最新执法行动中取得关键突破，凸显德国作为 SEP 执行核心法域的严厉态势，对未达成许可协议的实施者构成直接市场准入风险。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出口企业需警惕德国 SEP 诉讼的高禁令风险，务必提前评估 Wi-Fi 等标准专利许可状态。建议建立全球 FRAND 谈判应对机制，避免产品因单方禁令被排除出欧洲市场，同时关注韩国 NPE 的执法动态。

news_items.title: InterDigital inks renewed licence with Chinese business, likely Xiaomi
news_items.title_zh: InterDigital 与疑似小米的中国厂商续签许可
news_items.summary: US financial reporting documents show InterDigital renewed a “major Chinese vendor” and we know from its previous annual report that Xiaomi’s licence expired in 2025
news_items.content: US financial reporting documents show InterDigital renewed a “major Chinese vendor” and we know from its previous annual report that Xiaomi’s licence expired in 2025
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 美国 InterDigital 公司财务文件显示，其已与一家“主要中国供应商”续签专利许可协议。结合此前年报中关于小米许可于 2025 年到期的信息，媒体推测该被许可方极有可能是小米。此动态反映了全球 SEP 许可谈判的持续进展，虽未直接提及欧洲诉讼，但此类跨国许可通常涵盖包括欧洲在内的全球专利组合，对中欧贸易至关重要。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海企业应重视 SEP 全球布局，提前规划许可策略以避免诉讼风险。建议密切关注美欧财报披露中的许可动态，将其作为研判竞争对手策略及自身合规状况的重要情报来源，主动寻求 FRAND 原则下的合理解决方案。

news_items.ai_summary_zh: 本文探讨统一专利法院（UPC）成立后，标准必要专利（SEP）诉讼在欧洲进入新阶段。重点分析 UPC 在适用 FRAND 原则时的裁判趋势及其对全球许可谈判的影响。文章指出，UPC 正成为解决跨境 SEP 纠纷的核心平台，其判决将重塑欧洲乃至全球的专利许可格局。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临更集中的 SEP 诉讼风险，需提前布局 FRAND 合规证据。建议密切关注 UPC 判例，优化许可谈判策略，避免禁令风险，并利用统一程序降低多国应诉成本。

news_items.title: RAND licence battle in InterDigital against Amazon continues over Christmas
news_items.title_zh: InterDigital 诉亚马逊 RAND 许可战圣诞期间持续升级
news_items.summary: The UPC and Regional Court Munich confirm their ex parte AILI orders. The UK High Court maintains its jurisdiction to rule on a RAND rate determination in Amazon against InterDigital.
news_items.content:
Firms & Experts Sponsored: Firm Profiles Finnegan, Henderson, Farabow, Garrett & Dunner, LLP TALIENS PAUSTIAN & PARTNER View all Firm Profiles Sponsored: Expert Profiles Elisabeth Greiner Benoît Strowel Dr. Moritz Meckel View all Expert Profiles Sponsored Content UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP View all Sponsored Content

Finnegan, Henderson, Farabow, Garrett & Dunner, LLP

UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP

The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

The UPC and the Regional Court of Munich have confirmed their ex parte AILI orders, while at the same time the UK High Court maintains its jurisdiction to rule on a RAND rate determination on InterDigital's video streaming portfolio. These decisions have pleased Amazon and the SEP holder just before Christmas. However, these decisions, issued just before christmas, meant that both the implemntor and the SEP holder had positive news to take with them into the festive season.

The Unified Patent Court never sleeps. This analysis had already prevailed in 2024 and also applies to the Christmas season this year. Shortly before the holidays, the Mannheim local division under presiding judge Peter Tochtermann published its decision in Amazon’s appeal against an ex parte anti-interference licence injunction (AILI).

InterDigital had received this from both the local division and the Regional Court of Munich in a dispute over a licence for its video streaming portfolio at the end of September. At that time, both rulings from Mannheim and Munich prevented Amazon from seeking an interim licence in the UK that would have extended to UPC and continental European jurisdictions. Both courts held that compulsory interim licences undermine the fundamental right of patent enforcement.

On 22 December, the Mannheim local division not only rejected Amazon’s objection to the ex parte order and thus also confirmed the AILI. At the same time, the court also ordered an unusually high penalty if Amazon does not comply with the order.

The judgement of the local division states: “In case any or all of the Respondents disobey this Order, a penalty of up to € 50.000.000 may be set.”
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）曼海姆分院与慕尼黑地区法院确认针对亚马逊的单方反干扰许可禁令（AILI），并设定高额违令罚金。与此同时，英国高等法院坚持其对 InterDigital 视频流媒体组合进行 RAND 费率裁定的管辖权。双方当事人在节前分别获得有利裁决，凸显欧英两地并行诉讼的复杂格局。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临 UPC 禁令与英国费率诉讼双重风险。需警惕单方禁令突袭，提前布局抗辩策略；同时关注英欧管辖权冲突，合理规划全球许可谈判路径，避免高额罚金与不利费率裁定。

news_items.content: The eight most important takeaways from a dramatic 12 months in the F/RAND world
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 本文复盘了 2025 年标准必要专利领域的八大关键趋势，指出该年度在 FRAND 许可谈判与司法裁决上具有里程碑意义。内容涵盖统一专利法院（UPC）对全球费率管辖权的深化、欧盟监管框架的实际影响，以及主要司法辖区在禁令救济与反垄断交叉领域的最新动态，标志着欧洲 SEP 生态进入新阶段。

news_items.title: US firms secure Asian implementers interim licence against Nokia in the UK
news_items.title_zh: 美律所助亚洲企业在英获诺基亚临时许可
news_items.summary: Asus, Acer and Hisense have won the right to an interim licence following a UK High Court ruling last Thursday.
news_items.content:
Firms & Experts Sponsored: Firm Profiles BARDEHLE PAGENBERG NLO | European Patent & Trademark Attorneys BOEHMERT & BOEHMERT View all Firm Profiles Sponsored: Expert Profiles Nicola Dagg Daniel F. Seitz Dr. Peter Schindelmann View all Expert Profiles Sponsored Content UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP View all Sponsored Content

NLO | European Patent & Trademark Attorneys

UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP

The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Acer, Asus and Hisense have won interim licence dispute in the UK following a High Court ruling last Thursday. While Kirkland & Ellis represents Hisense, the decision marks the first major victory for Morgan Lewis and its clients Acer and Asus since the US law firm established its London patent litigation team.

The UK High Court has seen numerous FRAND and RAND rate determination and interim licence disputes recently, including Samsung versus ZTE, Warner Bros. and Paramount versus Nokia, and Amazon versus InterDigital.

Last Thursday, the UK High Court under presiding judge James Mellor granted an interim licence to Acer, Asus and Hisense (case IDs: HP-2025-000030, 000032, 000039 ).

The three Asian implementers obtained a declaration that a willing licensor in Nokia’s position would agree to an interim licence until the court determines “reasonable and non-discriminatory” terms for a patent licence.

The companies had requested that the UK High Court determine a RAND rate for Nokia’s video streaming portfolio. The case concerns Nokia’s portfolio of standard-essential patents (SEPs) declared essential to the H.264/AVC and H.265/HEVC video coding standards administered by the ITU-T. The standard is subject to Swiss law.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 英国高等法院裁定华硕、宏碁和海信有权获得针对诺基亚的临时许可。法官詹姆斯·梅勒确认，若许可方意愿合理，应在法院确定最终公平合理无歧视（FRAND）费率前授予临时许可。此案涉及诺基亚 H.264/H.265 视频编码标准必要专利组合，标志着美国律所在伦敦专利诉讼团队的首次重大胜利。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧遭遇 SEP 诉讼时，可积极利用英国法院寻求临时许可以维持运营。建议提前布局 FRAND 抗辩策略，并关注英美律所在伦敦的诉讼动态，灵活选择代理团队应对跨国知识产权纠纷。

news_items.ai_summary_zh: 中国科技巨头 OPPO 与德国汽车制造商奥迪达成蜂窝标准必要专利（SEP）许可协议。这是 OPPO 在汽车领域签署的第三份许可协议，奥迪作为 Avanci 专利池成员加入。该交易反映了欧洲汽车行业中 SEP 许可模式的持续扩展，表明主要车企正通过专利池解决通信技术标准授权问题，有助于减少潜在诉讼风险并推动行业标准化合作。

news_items.title: InterDigital and Bird & Bird challenge UK High Court jurisdiction in Amazon case
news_items.title_zh: InterDigital 挑战英国高院在亚马逊案中的管辖权
news_items.summary: Today the UK High Court judge Richard Meade hears InterDigital's jurisdictional challenge in the RAND-rate setting dispute.
news_items.content:
Firms & Experts Sponsored: Firm Profiles Kirkland & Ellis Carpmaels & Ransford COHAUSZ & FLORACK View all Firm Profiles Sponsored: Expert Profiles Dr. Regina Neufeind Dr. Johan Elkenbracht Mathias Ricker View all Expert Profiles Sponsored Content UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP View all Sponsored Content

UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP

The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

There is much at stake for Amazon today as UK High Court judge Richard Meade hears InterDigital's jurisdictional challenge in the RAND-rate setting dispute. If Meade denies his court's jurisdiction, it would deal a blow to the US internet giant.

The dispute between InterDigital and Amazon over a licence to InterDigital’s video streaming portfolio is still in its early stages, but the two opponents have already met several times in court. So far, the main issues have centred on anti-interim-licence injunctions and anti-suit injunctions at the UK High Court and the UPC’s Mannheim local division. The dispute has already attracted significant international attention.

However, the courts have yet to address the core issues: Is Amazon entitled to a FRAND rate set by the UK High Court? Is InterDigital entitled to injunctions in the UPC territory and Germany?

Today and tomorrow, the parties will again face off at the UK High Court. Judge Richard Meade will hear arguments on whether the court has jurisdiction to set a global FRAND rate for InterDigital’s portfolio, as Amazon has requested.

In late August 2025, Amazon sought a declaration of non-infringement at the High Court, alleging InterDigital had abused a dominant position. Amazon also sought declarations concerning InterDigital’s obligations under the ITU-T FRAND commitment (case ID: HP-2025-000043).

The US-based SEP owner is challenging the London court’s jurisdiction. Barristers Michael Bloch from Blackstone Chambers and Tom Jones from 8 New Square are representing InterDigital today in the Rolls Building courtroom on Fetter Lane.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 英国高院法官理查德·米德审理 InterDigital 提出的管辖权异议，争议焦点在于法院是否有权设定全球 FRAND 费率。该案同时涉及亚马逊在德国曼海姆地方法院及统一专利法院（UPC）的反诉禁令申请。若英国法院拒绝管辖，将重创亚马逊寻求全球费率裁决的战略，凸显英德两地司法辖区在标准必要专利争端中的博弈。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧遭遇 SEP 诉讼时，需警惕英德法院的平行程序风险。建议提前布局全球费率抗辩策略，关注英国法院管辖权趋势，并利用 UPC 机制平衡禁令压力，避免陷入多地诉讼困局。

news_items.title: SEP holders force UPC into FRAND dance with UK courts
news_items.title_zh: SEP 持有人迫使 UPC 介入并与英国法院展开费率管辖竞争
news_items.summary: Sun Patent has asked the UPC's Paris local division for a FRAND-rate determination in its dispute with Vivo.
news_items.content:
Firms & Experts Sponsored: Firm Profiles df-mp Patentanwälte Rechtsanwälte PartG mbB VOSSIUS & PARTNER Patentanwälte Rechtsanwälte mbB BOEHMERT & BOEHMERT View all Firm Profiles Sponsored: Expert Profiles Dr. Marcus Matuschek Nigel Stoate Dr Susanne Sonnenhauser View all Expert Profiles Sponsored Content UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP View all Sponsored Content

df-mp Patentanwälte Rechtsanwälte PartG mbB

VOSSIUS & PARTNER Patentanwälte Rechtsanwälte mbB

UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP

The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Sun Patent Trust has asked the UPC's Paris local division to set a FRAND rate in its dispute with Vivo. Ericsson has also filed a similar FRAND claim in its battle with Transsion. A FRAND-rate determination by the UPC could intensify competition with the UK High Court and Munich Regional Court.

Rumours have circulated among experts for some time. Since last Friday, it has become clear that the Unified Patent Court has received a complaint from an SEP holder regarding FRAND-rate determination.

At the end of November, the Court of Appeal rejected an application by implementer Vivo to halt the main proceedings in its dispute with Sun Patent Trust until the Court of Appeal decides whether the UPC has jurisdiction to make FRAND-rate determinations.

Sun Patent first applied to the UPC for a FRAND-rate determination in April. This forms part of two infringement actions brought by the Panasonic-affiliated licensing company against two SEPs at the Paris local division (case IDs: UPC_CFI_361/2025 and UPC_CFI_362/2025). Sun Patent aims to stop the sale of various 4G+ compatible Vivo devices.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: Sun Patent Trust 已向统一专利法院（UPC）巴黎本地分庭提起申请，请求裁定其与 Vivo 纠纷中的 FRAND 许可费率。此前爱立信也针对 Transsion 提出类似诉求。尽管英国上诉法院驳回 Vivo 暂停诉讼的申请，但 UPC 若确立费率裁决权，将与英国高等法院及慕尼黑地区法院形成直接管辖竞争，标志着欧洲 SEP 诉讼进入新阶段。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临更复杂的诉讼选择，需评估 UPC 与英德法院的策略差异。建议提前布局全球费率抗辩证据，关注 UPC 判例走向，避免因管辖权博弈陷入被动高额赔偿或禁售风险。
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Sun Patent 请求 UPC 巴黎分庭裁定涉 Vivo 案件的 FRAND 许可…", "UPC 介入费率裁决将加剧与英国及德国法院的管辖权竞争", "英国上诉法院拒绝暂停主程序，等待 UPC 管辖权最终确认"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕 UPC 成为 SEP 持有人新的择地行诉首选战场", "需统筹英、德、UPC 三地诉讼策略以优化全球和解筹码"]
news_items.ai_relevance_reason_zh: 涉及统一专利法院（UPC）首次受理标准必要专利费率裁决，重塑欧洲诉讼格局。

news_items.title: BREAKING: ACT-The App Association withdraws application to join ETSI
news_items.title_zh: 突发：应用协会 ACT 撤回加入 ETSI 申请

news_items.content: ACT to discuss “cooperation agreement” with the standard-setting body instead
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 应用协会（ACT）正式撤回加入欧洲电信标准协会（ETSI）的申请。此举标志着该代表中小型科技企业的组织改变策略，不再寻求成为 ETSI 正式成员，转而计划与标准制定机构商讨签署“合作协议”。这一动态反映了 SEP 领域利益相关方在参与标准制定机制上的策略调整，可能影响未来 FRAND 许可谈判的格局。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应对 SEP 纠纷时，需关注 ETSI 内部博弈变化。ACT 策略调整暗示非会员合作模式兴起，建议企业灵活利用多元渠道参与标准治理，优化许可谈判策略。
