news_items.title: UK Court of Appeal denies Tesla a pool licence
news_items.title_zh: 英国上诉法院驳回特斯拉获取专利池许可的上诉
news_items.summary: UK Court of Appeal denies Tesla pool licence in the car manufacturer's dispute with Avanci and its licensor InterDigital.
news_items.content:
Firms & Experts Sponsored: Firm Profiles WALLINGER RICKER SCHLOTTER TOSTMANN PAUSTIAN & PARTNER HOYNG ROKH MONEGIER View all Firm Profiles Sponsored: Expert Profiles Alexander Schlotter Marta Mendes Hosea Haag View all Expert Profiles Sponsored Content UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP View all Sponsored Content

WALLINGER RICKER SCHLOTTER TOSTMANN

UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP

The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

The UK courts have once again denied Tesla a 5G pool licence. The ruling was handed down today by the UK Court of Appeal in the car manufacturer's dispute with Avanci and its licensor InterDigital. But the judgment was not clear-cut.

The Court of Appeal panel comprising presiding judge Richard Arnold, Philippa Whipple and Stephen Phillips heard the appeal at the beginning of December. While Whipple and Phillips have now dismissed Tesla’s appeal, Arnold allowed it in part. Arnold believes the UK patent court has the authority to rule on a global FRAND rate for a pool licence.

As a consequence, the Court of Appeal dismissed the appeal in its entirety ( case ID: CA-2024-001749 ). According to the ruling, Tesla originally complained that Avanci has only offered a flat rate of $32 per vehicle for such a licence on a non-negotiable basis. Tesla contend that this rate far exceeds a FRAND rate.

Tesla had always confirmed that it needed a 5G licence in the UK. The UK is the fourth largest market for Tesla globally. Consequently, Tesla has undertaken to accept a FRAND licence on the terms determined by the patent court. Avanci and InterDigital have disputed the jurisdiction of the UK courts for determining a pool licence and have now prevailed with this position.

The Court of Appeal judges thus confirmed the first-instance ruling.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 英国上诉法院在特斯拉与 Avanci 及 InterDigital 的纠纷中，再次拒绝授予特斯拉 5G 专利池许可。尽管主审法官阿诺德认为英国法院有权裁定全球 FRAND 费率，但合议庭最终以多数意见驳回上诉，维持一审关于英国法院对此类专利池许可缺乏管辖权的裁定。特斯拉曾承诺接受法院确定的 FRAND 条款，但对方成功质疑了法院管辖权。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国车企出口欧洲时需警惕单一法域诉讼风险，英国法院对全球费率管辖权的收缩可能增加谈判难度。建议提前布局多国许可策略，避免依赖单一司法辖区解决全球 SEP 争议，并密切关注后续欧盟层面政策动向。

news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["英国管辖权受限迫使企业寻求多国并行诉讼或谈判策略", "固定高额许可费模式凸显事前合规与费率评估的重要性"]

news_items.title: It’s time to ask: Quo Vadis FRAND?
news_items.title_zh: 是时候发问：FRAND 原则将走向何方？
news_items.summary: Former in-house IP licensing executive, now independent IP consultant, Richard Buttrick revisits the current issues with SEP licensing and explores the emerging threats of geopolitics to the FRAND ecosystem
news_items.content: Former in-house IP licensing executive, now independent IP consultant, Richard Buttrick revisits the current issues with SEP licensing and explores the emerging threats of geopolitics to the FRAND ecosystem
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 前企业内部许可高管 Richard Buttrick 撰文重审标准必要专利（SEP）许可现状，指出当前 FRAND 生态系统正面临新兴的地缘政治威胁。文章探讨了在全球局势动荡背景下，传统公平、合理和无歧视原则如何受到挑战，以及这对欧洲乃至全球知识产权许可格局可能产生的深远影响。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧 SEP 布局需警惕地缘政治引发的许可规则波动。建议加强合规评估，建立多元化许可策略，并密切关注欧盟政策动向，以应对潜在的 FRAND 原则执行偏差风险。
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["资深专家重审 SEP 许可现状，指出 FRAND 机制面临新挑战", "地缘政治因素成为干扰全球 FRAND 生态系统稳定的新变量", "呼吁业界反思在地缘冲突下如何维持许可体系的公平与效率"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["地缘政治可能扭曲 FRAND 执行，企业需提升风险预警能力", "应构建灵活许可策略，适应欧洲 SEP 监管环境的不确定性"]
news_items.ai_relevance_reason_zh: 聚焦欧洲 SEP 许可与 FRAND 原则，涉及地缘政治对欧 IP 生态影响。

news_items.title: Ericsson breached FRAND obligations by pursuing injunctions against Lenovo, according to landmark UK ruling
news_items.title_zh: 英上诉法院裁定爱立信对联想寻求禁令违反 FRAND 义务
news_items.summary: Court of Appeal grants second short-term SEP licence declaration, following Panasonic decision
news_items.content: Court of Appeal grants second short-term SEP licence declaration, following Panasonic decision
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 英国上诉法院作出里程碑式裁决，认定爱立信在向联想寻求禁令救济时违反了 FRAND 义务。继松下案后，法院再次批准了短期标准必要专利许可声明。该判决明确了专利权人在谈判未决时申请禁令的界限，强化了被许可人的保护机制，对全球 SEP 许可实践具有深远指导意义。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧遭遇 SEP 诉讼时，可援引此案抗辩不当禁令。建议完善全球许可谈判策略，保留证据以证明对方违反 FRAND 义务，利用英国判例争取有利许可条件或阻止滥用禁令。
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院认定爱立信寻求禁令行为违反公平合理无歧视原则", "继松下案后再次批准短期标准必要专利许可声明", "明确 SEP 权利人在许可谈判期间申请禁令的法律边界"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["善用英国判例抗辩 SEP 权利人滥用禁令救济行为", "优化全球许可谈判策略并留存违反 FRAND 证据"]
news_items.ai_relevance_reason_zh: 英国上诉法院就 SEP 禁令与 FRAND 义务作出里程碑裁决，直接影响欧洲专利许可格局。

news_items.title: Landmark Optis-Apple global FRAND hearing kicks off at the English Court of Appeal
news_items.title_zh: Optis 诉苹果全球 FRAND 案在英国上诉法院开庭
news_items.summary: Optis is challenging the lower court’s issuing of an “anti-suit injunction” against a $300 million US court verdict
news_items.content: Optis is challenging the lower court’s issuing of an “anti-suit injunction” against a $300 million US court verdict
news_items.ai_summary_zh: Optis  Cellular Technology 就下级法院发出的“反诉禁令”向英国上诉法院提起挑战，该禁令旨在阻止苹果公司执行美国法院作出的 3 亿美元判决。此案标志着全球 FRAND 费率裁决管辖权之争的关键转折，英国法院正重新审视其在跨国知识产权争端中的干预边界与执行效力。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临 SEP 诉讼时，需警惕英国法院的全球费率管辖权及反诉禁令风险。建议提前布局多国诉讼策略，避免单一法域判决被全球强制执行，同时密切关注英国上诉法院对此类禁令的最新司法态度。
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Optis 挑战英法院针对美判决的反诉禁令，案情涉及巨额赔偿", "英国上诉法院开庭审理，聚焦全球 FRAND 裁决的管辖权冲突", "本案结果将界定英国法院在跨国 SEP 纠纷中的司法干预限度"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕英国法院全球管辖权，防范反诉禁令阻碍海外执行", "构建多国诉讼防御体系，避免单一判决引发全球连锁反应"]

news_items.title: Lessons about patent royalty rate reasonableness from the Nokia-Amazon battle
news_items.title_zh: 诺基亚诉亚马逊案对专利许可费率合理性的启示
news_items.summary: A data-driven analysis comparing HEVC asset quality indicates Nokia could charge Amazon a premium price in their global litigations
news_items.content: A data-driven analysis comparing HEVC asset quality indicates Nokia could charge Amazon a premium price in their global litigations
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 本文基于数据驱动分析，对比 HEVC 资产质量，指出诺基亚在全球诉讼中可向亚马逊主张溢价许可费。文章通过诺基亚与亚马逊的博弈，探讨了标准必要专利（SEP）在 FRAND 原则下费率合理性的判定逻辑，强调高质量专利组合在跨国 litigation 中的定价优势，为欧洲 SEP 争议解决提供新视角。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应对 SEP 诉讼时，应强化自身专利组合质量评估，利用数据证明技术贡献度。同时需警惕权利人借资产质量差异主张溢价，提前布局抗辩证据，避免在费率谈判中处于被动。
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["数据分析显示诺基亚 HEVC 专利资产质量优于行业平均水平", "高质量专利组合支持权利人在全球诉讼中主张更高许可费率", "案件揭示了 FRAND 原则下费率合理性判定的复杂性与证据依赖"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立专利资产质量量化评估体系，支撑费率谈判与诉讼抗辩", "关注欧洲法院对 FRAND 费率中“质量溢价”因素的裁判倾向"]
news_items.ai_relevance_reason_zh: 涉及欧洲 SEP 许可费率合理性及全球诉讼策略，具核心参考价值。

news_items.title: TCL v Access Advance and the lingering risk of jurisdictional disputes for patent pools
news_items.title_zh: TCL 诉 Access Advance 案与专利池管辖权风险
news_items.summary: Saturday Opinion: The unilateral determination of FRAND rates by a single court damage the entire patent pool system
news_items.content: Saturday Opinion: The unilateral determination of FRAND rates by a single court damage the entire patent pool system
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 本文评论 TCL 诉 Access Advance 一案，指出单一法院单方面裁定 FRAND 费率将破坏全球专利池体系。文章强调，若缺乏跨国协调，不同法域判决冲突将引发管辖权争端，增加许可不确定性，损害标准必要专利生态系统的稳定性与效率。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧参与 SEP 许可时，应警惕单一法域裁决风险，避免依赖单边诉讼结果。建议提前布局多国应诉策略，推动通过仲裁或双边协商确定全球费率，降低管辖权争议带来的合规与商业损失。
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["单一法院单边裁定 FRAND 费率危及专利池整体运作机制", "管辖权冲突可能导致全球许可策略陷入法律不确定性", "亟需建立跨国司法协调机制以维护 SEP 许可体系稳定"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["避免依赖单一国家法院裁定全球 FRAND 费率", "主动构建多国协同应对机制以规避管辖权冲突"]
news_items.ai_relevance_reason_zh: 涉及欧洲 SEP 许可费率裁决对专利池体系的冲击，关乎欧盟司法管辖权争议。

news_items.title: The UPC’s first two FRAND decisions – key lessons for SEP strategists
news_items.title_zh: 统一专利法院首批 FRAND 判决：对 SEP 策略师的关键启示
news_items.summary: The Unified Patent Court's first two FRAND decisions emphasise flexibility but are broadly favourable to patentees, argue Taylor Wessing's Michael Washbrook and Sheenal Singh
news_items.content: The Unified Patent Court's first two FRAND decisions emphasise flexibility but are broadly favourable to patentees, argue Taylor Wessing's Michael Washbrook and Sheenal Singh
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）作出首批两项涉及标准必要专利（SEP）的公平、合理及非歧视（FRAND）原则判决。尽管裁决强调程序灵活性，但整体趋势倾向于保护专利权人利益。该分析指出，这两项判决为 SEP 持有者及实施者在欧洲层面的许可谈判与诉讼防御提供了关键先例指引。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临更严峻的 SEP 诉讼环境，需警惕 UPC 倾向性裁判风险。建议提前梳理专利组合，优化 FRAND 谈判记录，并制定针对 UPC 程序的专项应诉策略以规避禁令风险。
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 首批 FRAND 判决确立灵活性但总体利好专利权人", "判决为欧洲 SEP 许可谈判与诉讼策略提供关键实务指引", "律所分析强调需重新评估在欧专利布局与侵权应对方案"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕 UPC 裁判倾向，提前完善 FRAND 谈判证据链", "针对 UPC 程序特性，制定专项应诉与专利布局策略"]
news_items.ai_relevance_reason_zh: 统一专利法院首批 FRAND 判决直接关乎欧洲标准必要专利诉讼策略与合规。

news_items.title: Proposed SEP regulation withdrawn following delays and EU mood shift
news_items.title_zh: 因延误与风向转变，欧盟拟议的 SEP 法规已被撤回
news_items.summary: The European Commission appears to have turned over a new leaf on tech and innovation, which has contributed to the major IP policy shift
news_items.content: The European Commission appears to have turned over a new leaf on tech and innovation, which has contributed to the major IP policy shift
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟委员会在科技与创新政策上出现重大转向，正式撤回了此前备受争议的标准必要专利（SEP）监管提案。这一决定源于立法进程的严重延误以及欧盟内部对加强行政干预态度的转变。该事件标志着欧盟在平衡专利权人与实施者利益时，暂时放弃了通过强制性注册和费率评估机制进行深度干预的路径，回归市场主导模式。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧 SEP 许可谈判中面临更灵活但也更不确定的环境。建议企业强化自身专利组合质量，利用 FRAND 原则进行个案博弈，同时密切关注后续可能出现的替代性行业指南或司法实践变化，避免过度依赖即将失效的行政救济预期。
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟委员会正式撤回拟议的标准必要专利监管法规提案", "政策转向主因是立法延误及欧盟内部对技术干预态度的变化", "欧盟暂时放弃建立强制性 SEP 透明度与费率评估行政机制"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["行政干预退坡，企业需回归基于 FRAND 原则的市场化谈判策略", "应重新评估在欧许可风险，加强高价值专利布局以应对不确定性"]
news_items.ai_relevance_reason_zh: 欧盟撤回 SEP 提案属重大政策转向，直接影响欧洲专利许可格局。

news_items.title: ETSI prepares for EU SEP regulation by appointing a chief policy officer
news_items.title_zh: ETSI 任命首席政策官备战欧盟标准必要专利监管
news_items.summary: Martin Chatel is newly appointed as chief policy officer at ETSI.
news_items.content:
Firms & Experts Sponsored: Firm Profiles TALIENS Meissner Bolte Patentanwälte Rechtsanwälte Partnerschaft mbB ARNOLD RUESS View all Firm Profiles Sponsored: Expert Profiles Birthe Struck Sebastian Schneller Liesbeth Weynants View all Expert Profiles Sponsored Content UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP View all Sponsored Content

Meissner Bolte Patentanwälte Rechtsanwälte Partnerschaft mbB

UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP

The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Martin Chatel has been newly appointed chief policy officer at ETSI.

Martin Chatel has been appointed chief policy officer at standards body ETSI. He took up the role on 10 February, which entails leading the policy affairs department. In its announcement yesterday, the standards body said Chatel “will spearhead ETSI’s strategic policy engagement with the European Commission” and other bodies, associations, policymakers and stakeholders, reporting to ETSI Director-General Jan Ellsberger. Chatel holds a masters in international law from Sciences Po Paris and an LLM from the Institute of European Studies.

He has previously held a variety of roles including senior IP legal services and product manager (patents) at IP firm Dennemeyer & Associate in Germany and manager of legal affairs at Cenelec (the European Electrotechnical Committee for Standardization) in Brussels. Ellsberger says, “The Policy Affairs Department will play a crucial role in supporting the European Union’s commitment to ethical, secure, and privacy-preserving technology deployment.” Chatel’s responsibilities are likely to include representing the interests of ETSI in the context of the EU Commission’s planned SEP reform, which will affect a large proportion of the patents in the ETSI standards.

One of the key proposals is a competence centre, which would carry out non-binding essentiality checks. Additionally, FRAND-rate determination by mediators would be mandatory, if non-binding, before parties can then litigate in UPC countries.

UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validity Test When EPO Opposition Is Pending 17 February 2026 by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Firms & Experts Sponsored: Firm Profiles Zimmermann & Partner Patentanwälte mbB Maiwald Intellectual Property PAUSTIAN & PARTNER View all Firm Profiles Sponsored: Expert Profiles Mari Korsten James G. Bell Dr. Johan Elkenbracht View all Expert Profiles Sponsored Content UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP View all Sponsored Content

Zimmermann & Partner Patentanwälte mbB

UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP

The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Video streaming is increasingly the focus of patent litigation. InterDigital is now suing the Walt Disney Company in the US, Brazil and Europe over patents protecting video compression technology.

6 February 2025 by Konstanze Richter

InterDigital claims the streaming platforms Disney+, ESPN+ and Hulu operated by the Walt Disney Company use advanced video compression that falls within the scope of InterDigital’s patents.

According to a press release , InterDigital has filed suits at the United States Federal District Court in the Central District of California (case ID: 2:2025cv00895 ), Rio de Janeiro State Court in Brazil, at Munich Regional Court in Germany, and at the Unified Patent Court’s local divisions in Mannheim and Düsseldorf.

In the US complaint, InterDigital claims it reached out to Disney in 2022 to request that the parties discuss the licensing of InterDigital’s patented technologies. But negotiations seemingly failed, as the claimant states that Disney is “still not authorised to use InterDigital’s patents”. As a result, the patent owner filed the suits against Disney.

news_items.title: Huawei, Qualcomm and Ericsson lead 5G race as industry shows no signs of slowing
news_items.title_zh: 华为高通爱立信领跑 5G，行业增速未减
news_items.summary: While SEP licensing in the smartphone market is mature, cellular IoT licensing is still in its infancy in many sectors. With the upcoming release of 5G Advanced and the initiation of 6G development, it is more crucial than ever for SEP holders and implementers alike to understand how to overcome licensing challenges.
news_items.content:
The 5G cellular standard is transforming industries, extending connectivity far beyond smartphones and computers into the IoT. From automotive and logistics to smart manufacturing, cities and even healthcare, 5G is driving a revolution that is reshaping entire sectors. Unlike any other technology, 5G is underpinned by thousands of SEPs.

With a dramatic rise in the number of 5G patent owners, 5G-declared patents and potential implementers compared to previous standards, the technology’s impact is extensive. It is redefining legal and business frameworks and transforming the SEP licensing and litigation landscape. While SEP licensing in the smartphone market is mature, cellular IoT licensing in many sectors is still in its infancy. As a result, 5G SEP licensing faces a long journey ahead, navigating geopolitical tensions, global disputes and local regulations that create significant challenges and uncertainty for both SEP owners and implementers.

The race for 5G patents has become more competitive than ever, with significant geopolitical implications as nations vie for leadership in critical technologies. Courts around the world are increasingly involved in setting global FRAND rates; Chinese courts are becoming particularly active and the UPC recently issued its first FRAND ruling. The determination of FRAND rates often includes, among other evidence, a ‘top-down’ approach, where the size of a 5G ultimate owner's portfolio is compared to the overall patent stack to calculate the 5G share. Further, courts are increasingly relying on robust patent data to assess the comparability of licence agreements, emphasising the importance of reliable and transparent data in these decisions.

The new 2025 5G patent report delivers a comprehensive analysis of patent portfolios, technical contributions and SEP market dynamics. Based on market-validated patent-declaration data, the report presents an authoritative ranking and patent portfolio shares of the world’s top innovators. It shows that the number of granted 5G patent declarations has more than doubled in the past three years, growing from slightly over 25,000 declared patent families in 2021 to more than 57,000 declared granted patent families as of October 2024. The leaders – Huawei, Qualcomm and Ericsson – dominate the top three positions in the 5G ranking. China-headquartered companies account for over 40% of 5G-declared patent families, while companies headquartered in Europe, South Korea and the United States each hold shares of 15 to 20%.

Huawei, Ericsson and Nokia lead in 5G-related technical standard submissions. By October 2024, the total number of these contributions had surpassed 80,000, marking an all-time high as companies continue to heavily invest in developing the technology. With the upcoming release of 5G Advanced in 2025 and the initiation of 6G development, the industry's momentum shows no sign of slowing down.

The value of patents – particularly SEPs – varies significantly, depending on their scope and utility. While some SEPs encompass foundational technologies that are applicable across multiple generations of standards, others may cover incremental enhancements to niche features with limited practical use. Consequently, simply counting patent families may not accurately reflect a portfolio’s true value or enforceability. A reliable indicator of a patent family’s value is the extent to which other innovations build upon the technology that it protects, and the breadth of the global protection deemed appropriate by its owner.

Based on the Patent Asset Index, there are notable shifts in 5G patent rankings compared to patent family counts. Qualcomm leads the rank, followed by Huawei and Samsung, which has moved up to third place. Significant advancements include InterDigital, rising from rank 17 to seven, reflecting its focus on R&D and SEP licensing. Similarly, the research lab of Shanghai Langbo has advanced from 52 to 23, Fraunhofer Institute has climbed from 27 to 24 and TNO Netherlands has risen from 95 to 54 – all emphasising the strength of R&D-centric organisations.

Notably, businesses specialising in patent acquisition, monetisation and enforcement show remarkable improvements in the Patent Asset Index ranking. For example, Pegasus Wireless, which acquired 5G patents from KT, has moved from 74 to 34. Longhorn IP, which acquired 5G portfolios from FG Innovation and Shanghai Langbo, has advanced its rank from 83 to 38 and Key Patent Innovations, which obtained a 5G portfolio from BlackBerry, has improved its position from 79 to 42.

5G IP Holdings, which owns a former FG Innovation portfolio, has climbed from 124 to 45. IP Bridge, which acquired 5G patents from Huawei and Panasonic, has risen from 141 to 47. New Radio, which owns 5G patents from FG Innovation, has moved from 106 to 48.

These shifts underscore that applying a value-based metric like the Patent Asset Index to 5G-declared patent data alters the landscape, favouring R&D-driven companies and those with rigorous patent acquisition and monetisation strategies. This approach highlights portfolios with higher value, providing a more meaningful assessment than raw patent counts alone.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 5G 标准必要专利许可在智能手机市场已成熟，但在物联网领域仍处起步阶段。随着 5G Advanced 发布及 6G 研发启动，全球专利声明量三年翻倍。统一专利法院（UPC）作出首项 FRAND 裁决，法院日益依赖“自上而下”法及可靠数据确定费率。华为、高通和爱立信占据领先地位，中国企业占比超四成，地缘政治与法律框架重塑许可格局。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临更严苛的 FRAND 费率审查与 UPC 诉讼风险。建议加强专利数据透明度以应对“自上而下”计算法，并密切关注 UPC 判例对全球许可策略的影响，提前布局物联网领域的合规许可谈判。

news_items.title: Why declaration data integrity is crucial for SEP stakeholders
news_items.title_zh: 为何声明数据完整性对 SEP 利益相关者至关重要
news_items.summary: Patent declaration databases provide critical insights into 4G and 5G patent ownership and are key resources for stakeholders involved in licensing negotiations, patent commercialisation and pool formation. Access to comprehensive data on declared patents, pooled assets and standards contributions is therefore essential to navigate these processes effectively.
news_items.content:
The ETSI declaration database contains over 555,000 declared patents spanning more than 100,000 patent families. In response to court rulings prohibiting enforcement of late-declared patents, many ETSI-declaring companies are adapting their strategies and patents are now often self-declared at the earliest possible stage, frequently including unpublished provisional or priority numbers.

The self-declaration process introduces significant variability when it comes to what is disclosed – some companies only provide provisional numbers, while others declare application and grant numbers as well as all jurisdictional counterparts. This lack of consistency results in an ambiguous ETSI database, which poses challenges for analysis and interpretation. So-called ‘top-down’ analysis to identify a 4G or 5G patent owner’s share can become inherently biased when based on unprocessed data as it can lead to underrepresenting the portfolio size of companies that share less information than others.

Over the past decade, patent data providers have developed sophisticated rules for matching, cleaning and normalising patent numbers, enabling them to identify and link many declared patents. However, the complexity of this process has deterred some data providers from fully committing to producing accurate data. As a result, several 5G patent reports have presented conflicting and often confusing findings, creating a distorted view of the true landscape.

For many 5G patent experts, the presence of multiple reports on 5G patent ownership with conflicting rankings is a cause for concern, particularly when reports consider publicly available ETSI-declared patents where all the data originates from the same public source. Both SEP owners and standards implementers – as well as IP lawyers and economists – are asking:

Which 4G or 5G patent report can be trusted?

Which one serves as the benchmark for the highest data quality?

How can we reconcile the differences between these studies?

While it is not transparent how certain reports conclude their patent shares, public rankings may have an impact on SEP licensing. More cases are incorporating the top-down approach for FRAND determinations, in which a patent owner’s portfolio size is compared to the total patent stack to calculate a 4G or 5G share. Additionally, when assessing the comparability of licence agreements, courts are increasingly relying on declaration data to determine whether an agreement is truly comparable. Reliable 4G and 5G patent data is playing an ever-growing role in SEP licensing and litigation, so declaration data integrity is becoming critical.

Matching various patent number formats

Companies submit patent numbers in various formats and types, leading to inconsistencies in matching ETSI-declared patent families. Figure 1 highlights the number of non-matched ETSI-declared patent families (INPADOC) by declaring company when relying solely on unprocessed patent number declarations.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: ETSI 数据库包含超 55 万项声明专利，但因企业自行申报格式不一导致数据混乱。这种不一致性使得基于原始数据的“自上而下”分析产生偏差，引发多份 5G 专利报告结论冲突。随着法院在 FRAND 裁决中日益依赖此类数据，确保声明数据的准确性与完整性已成为 SEP 许可和诉讼的关键基础。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧参与 SEP 许可或诉讼时，需警惕单一报告的偏差。建议建立内部专利数据清洗能力，交叉验证 ETSI 原始数据，避免因数据源缺陷导致份额被低估或在 FRAND 谈判中处于劣势。

news_items.title: LexisNexis launches new tool for cleaner SEP data
news_items.title_zh: LexisNexis 推出新工具以净化 5G 标准必要专利数据
news_items.summary: LexisNexis launches tool to clean data for 5G patents to aid in licensing negotiations and FRAND-rate determination.
news_items.content:
Firms & Experts Sponsored: Firm Profiles Zimmermann & Partner Patentanwälte mbB NLO | European Patent & Trademark Attorneys BOEHMERT & BOEHMERT View all Firm Profiles Sponsored: Expert Profiles Muazzez Korutürk Dr. Daniel Herrmann Penny Gilbert View all Expert Profiles Sponsored Content UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP View all Sponsored Content

Zimmermann & Partner Patentanwälte mbB

NLO | European Patent & Trademark Attorneys

UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP

The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

The US information and data service company LexisNexis has launched a new tool to clean up data for 5G patents. The tool should aid companies for issues such as licensing negotiations and FRAND-rate determination. However, it is unlikely to solve important problems such as the over declaration of SEPs or the essentiality check.

LexisNexis Legal & Professional has launched the ‘Cellular Verified’ initiative. According to the company , through a year-long collaboration with major SEP holders in the telecoms sector, LexisNexis was able to achieve 99.9% data accuracy by matching, cleaning, enhancing, and verifying its database of patent families with the data in the ETSI database.

The European Telecommunications Standards Institute is the world’s most important institute for the declaration of standard essential patents. Patent holders can introduce their patents into a standard via the institute. The problem, however, is that ETSI does not check whether the patents are really relevant to the standard. It also does not determine the patents’ economic value, which often leads to lawsuits if the companies cannot agree on a rate for a licence.

The initiative also addresses inconsistent data. “Patent declaration data is inherently inconsistent, shaped by each company’s unique declaration practices,” says analyst Tim Pohlmann from LexisNexis Intellectual Property Solutions.

news_items.title: EU takes another swipe at Chinese SEP approach with new WTO challenge
news_items.title_zh: 欧盟就中国 SEP 全球费率裁定向 WTO 提起新挑战
news_items.summary: China’s global rate-setting contrary to TRIPS says European Commission, which has also objected to anti-suit injunctions issued by the country’s courts
news_items.content: China’s global rate-setting contrary to TRIPS says European Commission, which has also objected to anti-suit injunctions issued by the country’s courts
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟委员会正式向世界贸易组织（WTO）提出挑战，指控中国法院设定的全球标准必要专利（SEP）费率违反《TRIPS 协定》。欧方特别反对中国法院颁发的反诉禁令，认为此举干扰了外国司法管辖权及公平合理的许可谈判。该行动标志着中欧在 SEP 监管与司法管辖权上的冲突升级，欧盟试图通过多边贸易机制遏制其认定的保护主义司法实践。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临更严苛的 SEP 合规审查与诉讼风险。建议企业避免依赖单一法域的全球费率裁定，需构建多国并行诉讼策略，并密切关注 WTO 程序进展对 FRAND 原则解释的潜在影响。
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟指控中国法院设定全球 SEP 费率违反 TRIPS 协定义务", "欧方明确反对中国法院针对外国诉讼颁发的反诉禁令措施", "欧盟选择通过 WTO 争端解决机制而非双边对话处理此 IP 冲突"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需警惕欧盟利用 WTO 规则限制中国司法管辖权的长期趋势", "应优化全球许可谈判策略，减少对反诉禁令等救济措施的依赖"]
news_items.ai_relevance_reason_zh: 欧盟委员会就中国 SEP 费率裁定向 WTO 提起挑战，直接涉及欧洲核心 IP 政策与国际贸易规则。
news_items.title_en: EU takes another swipe at Chinese SEP approach with new WTO challenge

news_items.title: Four things to know about Samsung’s counteroffensive in SEP dispute with KPN
news_items.title_zh: 三星在 SEP 争端中对 KPN 发起反制的四大关键点
news_items.summary: Though KPN already won $341 million in litigation with Samsung, the Korean electronics giant now seeks a declaratory judgment of non-infringement of cellular SEPs
news_items.content: Though KPN already won $341 million in litigation with Samsung, the Korean electronics giant now seeks a declaratory judgment of non-infringement of cellular SEPs
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 尽管荷兰电信商 KPN 此前在与三星的诉讼中获赔 3.41 亿美元，但三星近期发起反击，寻求法院宣告其蜂窝标准必要专利未侵权。本文分析了此次反制行动的四个关键维度，揭示了双方在 FRAND 原则下的博弈新动态及全球 SEP 诉讼策略的演变趋势。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧遭遇 SEP 诉讼时，应借鉴三星“反制”策略，主动寻求不侵权宣告。同时需警惕高额赔偿风险，建立完善的 FRAND 合规抗辩体系，利用多国管辖差异优化全球诉讼布局。
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["三星寻求不侵权宣告判决以扭转此前败诉局面", "KPN 虽获巨额赔偿但面临新的法律不确定性挑战", "案件折射出全球 SEP 许可谈判与诉讼策略的复杂性"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["主动提起不侵权宣告之诉可掌握诉讼主动权", "构建跨法域 FRAND 合规策略以应对高额索赔风险"]
news_items.ai_relevance_reason_zh: 涉及欧洲 SEP 许可纠纷及跨国诉讼策略，对欧企合规具参考价值。
news_items.title_en: Four things to know about Samsung’s counteroffensive in SEP dispute with KPN

news_items.title: Patent pool administrator calling for 3D audio licensors
news_items.title_zh: 专利池管理员呼吁 3D 音频技术权利人加入许可计划
news_items.summary: Although the Alliance for Open Media's IAMF specification is purportedly royalty-free, those terms likely will face backlash considering Vectis IP is asking patent holders to reach out regarding a potential IAMF pool
news_items.content: Although the Alliance for Open Media's IAMF specification is purportedly royalty-free, those terms likely will face backlash considering Vectis IP is asking patent holders to reach out regarding a potential IAMF pool
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 尽管开放媒体联盟声称其 IAMF 规范免收专利费，但专利池管理机构 Vectis IP 正积极联系专利权人，筹划建立潜在的 IAMF 专利池。这一举动预示着该标准的免费条款可能面临挑战，反映出在 3D 音频领域，标准必要专利的许可博弈正在加剧，免费承诺与实际专利主张之间存在张力。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国音视频企业需警惕 IAMF 标准从免费转向收费的风险。建议提前排查自身技术是否落入潜在专利池范围，评估许可成本，并关注 Vectis IP 动向，做好应对 FRAND 许可谈判或潜在诉讼的准备。
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Vectis IP 正招募权利人构建潜在 IAMF 专利池", "IAMF 规范的免版税声明恐遭专利池挑战", "D 音频领域标准必要专利许可局势趋紧"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕免版税标准转为收费专利池的合规风险", "提前布局应对潜在 SEP 许可谈判与诉讼策略"]
news_items.ai_relevance_reason_zh: 涉及欧洲专利池运营及 SEP 许可动态，虽无具体法域但影响欧洲市场。

news_items.title: ZTE fires back with Chinese/UPC/German complaints following Samsung’s UK FRAND suit
news_items.title_zh: 中兴回应三星英国诉讼，发起中德及 UPC 专利投诉
news_items.summary: Exclusive: Chinese patentee has filed slew of SEP cases after being targeted in London global rate-setting action
news_items.content: Exclusive: Chinese patentee has filed slew of SEP cases after being targeted in London global rate-setting action
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 针对三星在英国提起的全球费率裁决诉讼，中国专利权人中兴通讯迅速反击，在中国、德国法院及统一专利法院（UPC）提起一系列标准必要专利侵权诉讼。此举标志着中欧科技巨头在 SEP 许可争端中，正利用欧洲多地司法管辖权进行复杂的跨境法律博弈，凸显全球费率诉讼引发的连锁反应。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临 SEP 诉讼时，应善用 UPC 及成员国法院构建防御体系。面对英国全球费率诉讼，需提前布局多国平行诉讼以增加谈判筹码，避免陷入被动接受单一法域裁决的局面，同时密切关注 FRAND 原则在不同辖区的适用差异。
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["三星在英国发起全球费率诉讼后，中兴立即启动反制措施", "中兴同步在中国、德国及统一专利法院提起多项专利侵权投诉", "案件凸显 SEP 争端中多法域并行诉讼与全球费率裁决的冲突"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用 UPC 及多国诉讼策略，平衡英国全球费率诉讼风险", "建立跨国 IP 应急机制，针对不同法域特性制定差异化应对方案"]
news_items.ai_relevance_reason_zh: 涉及中欧企业在欧多国及 UPC 的 SEP 诉讼对抗，属核心情报。
